races.race_name: Kildare Hunt Club Cross Country Chase for the Ladies Perpetual Cup

runners.name: Willitgoahead
runners.spotlight: Mixed form in the top hunter chases last season; stand-out effort third behind Wonderwall and Its On The Line at Cheltenham; started this season brightly with second to Its On The Line at Down Royal; unseated mid-race at Thurles; pulled up at Cheltenham; major chance if he adapts to the demands of this circuit; cheekpieces fitted; good 7lb claimer.
runners.comment: High-class hunter chaser, questions to answer, major chance if he adapts to this circuit

runners.name: Fountain House
runners.spotlight: Overcame two spots of bother when a winning favourite in last season's Bishopscourt Cup here over the regular track; well beaten in two orthodox hunter chases this season; in contrast, has won nine consecutive races in points, including three this term; was relatively immature when beaten by Knockiel Synge in this race in 2024; a leading points rider claims a valuable 7lb.
runners.comment: Second in this race in 2024, has an outstanding record in points, top rider claims 7lb

runners.name: Found A Star
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced maiden point-to-pointer; nothing to suggest she can play a featuring role.
runners.comment: Lightly raced maiden point-to-pointer, nothing to suggest she can play a featuring role

runners.name: Grange Soldier
runners.spotlight: Nothing of note in his form since winning a 3m Tipperary novice hunter chase almost two years ago; struggled when one of the rank outsiders in a stronger race than this over this course in February.
runners.comment: Nothing of note in his form since winning a 3m Tipperary novice hunter chase win in 2024

runners.name: Empire Soldier
runners.spotlight: Only one win from 13 starts in points; has failed to complete this course in two attempts; same story over the banks at Lingstown in a race won by Fountain House.
runners.comment: Has failed to complete this course in two attempts, same story over the banks at Lingstown

runners.name: Samdegoda
runners.spotlight: Maiden in points, placed four times; pulled up at Limerick on his only hunter chase start.
runners.comment: Maiden in points, placed four times, pulled up at Limerick on his only hunter chase start

runners.name: Keep On Dreaming
runners.spotlight: Shaped well at Tipperary last May in his first hunter chase; failed to match that form in races at Limerick and Thurles during the winter; form of his point-to-point wins does not amount to much; however, he is sure to know his job coming from this source.
runners.comment: Form of his point-to-point wins does not amount to much, sure to have been well schooled

runners.name: Turnupdevolume
runners.spotlight: Coped well with the demands of this course when second in this event 12 months ago, 2l in front of the 2024 winner Knockiel Synge; has failed to show a similar standard of form in points this season; however, signs that he may be coming right again when fourth to Fountain House at Lingstown.
runners.comment: Second in this event 12 months ago, fourth to Fountain House at Lingstown, place chance

runners.name: Knockiel Synge
runners.spotlight: Veteran pointer, two wins and 14 places from 35 starts; enjoyed a career highlight when beating Fountain House in this race two seasons ago; confirmed his liking for this challenge by finishing third in last year, 2l behind runner-up Turnupdevolume; out of sorts in points this season; pulled up on last three starts, including in a Lingstown banks race won by Fountain House.
runners.comment: Beat Fountain House in this race in 2024, third last year, has been out of sorts in points

races.race_name: Albert Bartlett Triple Crown Series Final Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: San Hilario
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden over hurdles; runner up in five of his last six starts, including in handicaps here at 2m3f and 2m5f; beaten 9.5l in maidens the last twice at Navan and Clonmel; place shout returned to handicap company.
runners.comment: 0-10, runner up in five of last six inc' twice here, place shout back in handicap company

runners.name: Moab
runners.spotlight: British challenger; dual-purpose; has won on the AW on the Flat but is 0-10 over hurdles; went close off 1lb lower at Huntingdon (20.5f, soft) in January; ground may have been too lively at Southwell latest; could outrun his odds back on easier ground.
runners.comment: Went close off 1lb lower at Huntingdon in Jan', ground too quick latest, could outrun odds

runners.name: Sticwiththeprocess
runners.spotlight: Justified short odds to win a Fairyhouse maiden hurdle in November (2m, sft-hvy); placed in both handicap hurdle starts since, over this C&D (sft-hvy) and back at Fairyhouse (2m4f, yld-sft); new blinkers could eke out more; player.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdle winner, placed in both h'cap starts since, blinkers could eke out more

runners.name: Kobalt St Georges
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; won over C&D in January (heavy) off 90; has come up short twice since, in a listed handicap chase at Naas and back over hurdles at Cork last time; 2lb wrong and others make more appeal.
runners.comment: C&D winner in January, beaten off revised mark latest, others appeal more; reserve

runners.name: Daylatedollarshort
runners.spotlight: British raider has won his first two starts in handicap company, by a neck at Wetherby (21.5f, soft) and by 2.25l over the same C&D on good ground off 4lb higher; has gone up another 7lb but he's unexposed and looks progressive.
runners.comment: 2-2 since tackling handicaps, up 7lb for 2.25l win latest, unexposed and progressive

runners.name: Another High Five
runners.spotlight: Scottish raider; 0-5 but getting closer with each run this season; 2.5l behind Judicial Deference on penultimate at Kelso (22.5f, heavy); beaten a neck at Hexham on latest (2m4f, soft); cheekpieces go on now; can't discount.
runners.comment: Scottish raider, good placed efforts last twice, can't discount in first-time cheekpieces

runners.name: Onefortheditch
runners.spotlight: 3-26 over hurdles; runner up the last twice, off 5lb lower at Leopardstown and then off 3lb lower at Cork when 1.5l behind Onlymammycanloveme; weighted to turn that around but may be vulnerable to less exposed sorts.
runners.comment: 3-26 over hurdles, runner up the last twice, may be vulnerable to less exposed sorts

runners.name: Assertive Walk
runners.spotlight: Maiden has run his best two races on good ground; beaten 7.75l when fourth behind a couple of these in a qualifier for this race at Wetherby last time; likely capable of better and can't discount.
runners.comment: Maiden, behind a couple of these at Wetherby latest, likely capable of better
runners.owner: Mrs P Bond, Magniers, Mrs P Shanahan

runners.name: Judicial Deference
runners.spotlight: British raider is 3-5 over hurdles; won his maiden at Musselburgh (2m4f, gd-sft) and then added handicaps at Haydock (2m5f, gd-sft) and Kelso (22.5f, heavy); brought down four out when still going okay at Kelso latest; could go well.
runners.comment: 3-5 over hurdles, still going okay when brought down on latest, could go well

runners.name: Laughing John
runners.spotlight: British challenger is 2-2 since tackling handicaps; won a three runner race at Doncaster (16.5f, gd-sft) off 106 then won in emphatic style at Wincanton upped to 2m4f (gd-sft) off 2lb higher; hiked up 10lb and moves up in grade but could be more to come; cheekpieces go on.
runners.comment: 2-2 in handicap hurdles, up 10lb for easy win latest, could be more to come; cheekpieces

runners.name: Pat's Legacy
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on handicap debut at Down Royal (20.5f, yielding) on latest off 111 (Accustomed 0.75l behind in second); raised 6lb for that and these are deeper waters; cheekpieces go on.
runners.comment: Won on h'cap debut on latest, up 6lb and these are deeper waters, cheekpieces go on

runners.name: Almuhit
runners.spotlight: Four-time Flat winner is 2-19 over hurdles; 8l behind Laughing John when runner up in qualifier for this at Wincanton on penultimate; modest run on the level at Musselburgh on latest; others make more appeal.
runners.comment: 2-19, 8l behind Laughing John in qualifier, modest Flat run latest, others preferred

runners.name: Krabat
runners.spotlight: Three-time chase winner is 0-23 over hurdles; rated 12lb lower in this sphere but failed to exploit his lesser mark on last start over timber at Gowran; cheekpieces need to eke out more.
runners.comment: Three-time chase winner, 0-23 over hurdles, new cheekpieces need to eke out more

runners.name: Powerful
runners.spotlight: Won on handicap debut here (2m, heavy) in November; won again off his revised mark of 120 here in January (2m5f, sft-hvy); drop back to 2m didn't suit at Fairyhouse latest; better expected over this longer trip; chance.
runners.comment: Dual course winner, drop to 2m against him latest, chance stepped back up in trip

runners.name: Matt Connor
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; has won three of his last four, two over hurdles and on the Flat at Gowran on latest; raised 11lb for his latest win over timber at Naas (18.5f, heavy) in January; this is harder but he did that easily and he is ground versatile; player.
runners.comment: Dual purpose, up 11lb for Naas win in January, Gowran Flat win latest, player

runners.name: Accustomed
runners.spotlight: Good run when a 3.5l sixth at Leopardstown in December and narrowly denied on his next start at Down Royal (20.5f, yielding); pulled up on latest at Wexford, however, and needs to bounce back in first-time blinkers; 2lb wrong.
runners.comment: Narrowly denied on penultimate, pulled up latest, blinkers on, 2lb wrong

runners.name: Onlymammycanloveme
runners.spotlight: Arrives here in top form after winning back to back handicap hurdles at Cork over 2m4f (Onefortheditch 1.5l behind in second) and 3m; has gone up a total of 14lb and this is tougher but can't discount in current form.
runners.comment: Up 14lb for back to back wins at Cork, this tougher but can't discount in current form

runners.name: Freshers Week
runners.spotlight: British challenger won a novice at Market Rasen on his second start; third off this mark in both handicaps, over 24.5f at Haydock and 21.5f at Wetherby; needs to find a bit more.
runners.comment: Market Rasen novice winner, beaten off this mark in first two h'caps, needs more

runners.name: Fairlander
runners.spotlight: Much improved effort on handicap debut at Down Royal in March when a 1.25l third of 19; this much tougher and he's 2lb wrong but he's unexposed and can't be totally discounted.
runners.comment: Much improved on h'cap debut latest, this tougher and 2lb wrong but can't dismiss
runners.owner: David L'Estrange & Eric Newnham & William Esdaile

runners.name: Bright Legend
runners.spotlight: Flat winner is 1-17 over hurdles, the win coming in a Sedgefield maiden on latest (2m4f, good); previously proved no match for Laughing John in a handicap at Wincanton and work to do to turn that form around in new headgear.
runners.comment: 1-17 over hurdles, no match for Laughing John on penultimate, new headgear combo

runners.name: Moylussa
runners.spotlight: 1-9 over hurdles; attempted to make all when finishing behind a couple of these at Cork latest (2m4f, yielding); probably up against it in this company and might prefer softer ground than this.
runners.comment: 1-9 over hurdles, behind a couple of these at Cork latest, might prefer softer ground

runners.name: Whatsavailable
runners.spotlight: First reserve; all three previous wins at Fairyhouse; back to form when a 3.75l third behind Onlymammycanlove me at Cork on latest; probably vulnerable to younger legs.
runners.comment: Three-time Fairyhouse winner, back to form latest but vulnerable in this company; reserve

runners.name: Must Go Now
runners.spotlight: 1-16 over hurdles; ran well when a close third at Cork off 1lb higher on penultimate (2m4f, yielding); needs to bounce back from disappointing Fairyhouse run latest; others look better treated.
runners.comment: 1-16 over hurdles, needs to bounce back from poor Fairyhouse run on latest

runners.name: Rakki
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in a Roscommon maiden hurdle last September (15.5f, gd-yld); respectable run on handicap debut here in November (19.5f, heavy) but disappointed in a stronger race at Limerick (2m, sft-hvy) when last seen; may do better back on a sounder surface.
runners.comment: Roscommon maiden hurdle winner, below best latest, better ground could help

runners.name: Moon Mission
runners.spotlight: 0-5 over hurdles; best run yet on handicap debut here on latest when a 3.5l third of 15 behind Powerful (2m5f, sft-hvy); 3lb rise for that seems a touch harsh and not sure this drop in trip will suit.
runners.comment: 0-5 over hurdles, 3.5l behind Powerful here latest (2m5f), up 3lb, drop in trip a query
runners.owner: J T O'Connor & M P Gleeson & P M Fortune & A McGonigle

runners.name: Union Park
runners.spotlight: Three-time chase winner is 2-29 over hurdles; rated 13lb lower over timber but recent form in both codes has been disappointing and a major revival is needed.
runners.comment: 2-29 over hurdles, recent form disappointing, major revival needed

runners.name: Grandero Bello
runners.spotlight: Has come up dry since winning a handicap chase at Galway in October 2024; soundly beaten all four starts this term; won his maiden hurdle here but others arrive in better form.
runners.comment: No win since October 2024, well beaten all four starts this season, others preferred

races.race_name: Killashee Hotel Handicap Hurdle (Listed Race)

runners.name: Davy Crockett
runners.spotlight: Looked a smart prospect last year but has failed to deliver in three runs this term, although was highly tried in his first two runs of 2026; never figured on handicap debut at Fairyhouse latest; down 2lb and tongue tie now reached for.
runners.comment: Yet to build on early promise, soundly beaten on h'cap debut latest, tongue tie tried now
runners.owner: John P McManus & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Khrisma
runners.spotlight: British raider; dual Grade 3 bumper winner in France; won two of first four starts over hurdles for this yard but a bad mistake two out proved costly when pulled up on handicap debut at Kelso latest; could go well if puts in a clear round of jumping.
runners.comment: Bad mistake costly on handicap bow at Kelso latest, could go well if jumps better

runners.name: Touch Me Not
runners.spotlight: Talented chaser rated 152 in that sphere and a Grade 2 novice chase winner at this venue; soundly beaten in the Grand Annual on latest and now tries to exploit a 17lb lower hurdles mark on first start over timber since April 2024; can't discount.
runners.comment: Grade 2 novice winner here over fences, rated 17lb lower over hurdles, can't discount

runners.name: Mode Avion
runners.spotlight: French bumper winner; 4yo won easily on handicap bow at Fairyhouse in February (16.5f, heavy); came up short off 14lb higher back there on latest when 1lb wrong and similar story here; would probably prefer softer ground.
runners.comment: Won easily on h'cap debut at Fairyhouse, beaten off revised mark latest, wants it softer

runners.name: Putapoundinthejar
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; 2-8 over hurdles, his last win coming at this meeting last year off 7lb lower; no impact over timber since but won on the Flat at Bellewstown 17 days ago and Sean Bowen was on board for that win here last season; can't discount.
runners.comment: Dual-purpose, won at this meeting last year under Bowen, Flat win latest, can't discount

runners.name: Full Of Life
runners.spotlight: Handicap debutante; 2-7 over hurdles and made all to land a Grade 3 mares' novice at Down Royal on her penultimate start (2m1f, gd-yld); well beaten in mares' novice at Cheltenham on latest; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: 2-7 over hurdles, Gr' 3 mares' novice winner, poor at Cheltenham latest, needs to rebound

runners.name: We're Red And Blue
runners.spotlight: Has held form well since scoring at Huntingdon in December off 9lb lower; good third in Imperial Cup on penultimate and 4l runner up at Haydock off 1lb lower on latest; in the mix again with cheekpieces an interesting addition.
runners.comment: Held form well since Huntingdon win inc' third in Imperial Cup, in the mix; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Arch Empire
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; 1-10 over hurdles; soundly beaten off 2lb higher when last seen at Down Royal in November; others make more appeal.
runners.comment: 1-10 over hurdles, well beaten when last seen in November, others appeal more

runners.name: Autumn Twist
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; maiden and novice hurdle winner has yet to fire in handicaps; pulled up when 40-1 at Cork on latest; others hold more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Maiden and novice hurdle winner, yet to fire in h'caps, pulled up latest, others preferred

runners.name: Murcia
runners.spotlight: Grade 1 Juvenile winner at Aintree (2m1f, gd-sft) last year; has come up dry since, beaten similar distances in first three handicaps; dropped 1lb for an 11l fifth behind Kalix Delabarriere on latest and Townend takes over now; drying ground a plus and could go well.
runners.comment: Grade 1 winner last year, no joy since but drying ground a plus, the choice of Townend

runners.name: Messerschmitt
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; 1-10 over hurdles; good effort at big odds when a 4.5l fourth of 18 in the Imperial Cup at Sandown in March; didn't back that up at Fairyhouse on latest; would have a place chance with a repeat of that Sandown run.
runners.comment: Good effort in Imperial Cup on penultimate, place chance if repeats that form

runners.name: I'm Slippy
runners.spotlight: Won his first two starts, a bumper at Fairyhouse last April and a Cork maiden hurdle on his seasonal return in November; 7.5l second in Fairyhouse Grade 3 later that month but soundly beaten in three runs since, including in handicaps last twice; more needed.
runners.comment: Progress has stalled since back-to-back wins and Gr' 3 place, soundly beaten last thrice

runners.name: Karbau
runners.spotlight: Arguably a shade unlucky when a beaten favourite in the County Hurdle but rider reported that he stopped quickly when soundly beaten at Fairyhouse last time; Townend jumps ship; needs to rediscover best.
runners.comment: Beaten fav' the last twice, reportedly stopped quickly on latest, Townend jumps ship

runners.name: Balko d'Ange
runners.spotlight: Won off 8lb lower at Navan in November and good third at Leopardstown off revised mark the following month; has fallen in two of last three starts, however, so he's a risky proposition for now.
runners.comment: Won off 8lb lower in Nov', has fallen two of last three starts, risky proposition for now

runners.name: Kalix Delabarriere
runners.spotlight: Back to form with a bang at Fairyhouse under this rider latest, winning by 5l with a few of these behind; a 9lb rise for that seems fair but overall profile suggests he's far from guaranteed to back that up; obvious chance if he does.
runners.comment: Back to form with a bang at Fairyhouse under this rider latest, obvious chance with repeat

runners.name: Lord Erskine
runners.spotlight: Admirable dual-purpose veteran is a winner of 14 races; 6-34 over hurdles but time may be catching up with him based on the evidence of his last two runs and was well beaten in this in 2024 off 2lb lower.
runners.comment: Admirable dual-purpose veteran, time may be catching up on evidence of last two runs

runners.name: Jet To Monte Carlo
runners.spotlight: Promise in maiden hurdles and ran out an easy winner on handicap debut at Fairyhouse three weeks ago (2m, yld-sft); raised 9lb and this is tougher but he's unexposed and there could be plenty more to come.
runners.comment: Raised 9lb for easy handicap debut win at Fairyhouse, unexposed, may be more to come

runners.name: Rockymountainbleu
runners.spotlight: Course winner in decent form and has finished in the first four in four of five starts this season; beaten 3.25l behind Sainte Lucie at Cork on latest; cheekpieces worn that day left off and he's 4lb wrong here; minor place claims.
runners.comment: Course winner, in decent form, beaten 3.25l on latest, 4lb wrong here, minor place shout

runners.name: Washington
runners.spotlight: Won well on seasonal return at Chepstow in October off 128 and 1lb lower now but he's suffered heavy defeats on his four starts since that success; leap of faith required to back him here.
runners.comment: Won at Chepstow off 1lb higher in Oct', heavy defeats four starts since, hard to fancy

runners.name: Larzac
runners.spotlight: Better effort on handicap debut at Cork (2m3f, yielding) on latest, travelling strongly before weakening in the closing stages; 1lb wrong here but it's early days and this shorter trip should suit; player.
runners.comment: Improved effort on h'cap bow latest, this shorter trip a plus, 1lb wrong but a player

runners.name: Frankendael
runners.spotlight: No joy since winning a listed handicap hurdle at Listowel in September 2024 off 6lb lower; 1lb wrong here and lacks a recent run; rider's claim a plus but others make more appeal.
runners.comment: No win since Sept' 2024, off since last June, 1lb wrong, rider's claim a plus

races.race_name: PRL Champion Novice Hurdle (Grade 1)

runners.name: Skylight Hustle
runners.spotlight: One of three for stable; point winner; beaten in sole bumper and on hurdles debut at Gowran in early November; off the mark in a maiden at Fairyhouse later that month and took the step up to Grade 1 company in his stride at Leopardstown's Christmas meeting, winning by 5l; beaten when falling in the 2m5f Turners at Cheltenham; this trip more suitable and could bounce back in the first time hood.
runners.comment: Point, maiden hurdle and Gr' 1 winner, trip too far on latest, could rebound; hooded

runners.name: El Cairos
runners.spotlight: One of three for yard; smart bumper form; fell with race at his mercy on hurdles debut at Leopardstown in December; survived a final flight blunder to win a maiden at Thurles; didn't get home up the Cheltenham hill in the Supreme last time; this track should suit better; preferred by Kennedy and big chance if cutting out the jumping errors.
runners.comment: Didn't get home in the Supreme, this track more suitable, big chance if jumps well

runners.name: Koktail Brut
runners.spotlight: One of three for yard; bright start over timber, winning a C&D maiden (heavy) and beating Blake by a head in the Royal Bond; limitations seemingly exposed in Grade 1 company on his next three runs but back on song with a Grade 2 win at Fairyhouse last time (left in lead after Blake fell at the last); wouldn't want the track to dry out too much.
runners.comment: Dual Grade 2 winner, closely matched with Blake, drying track would be a slight worry

runners.name: Sober
runners.spotlight: One of three for yard; Group 2 winner on the Flat for Andre Fabre and won the Queen Alexandra for this stable at Ascot; bright start over hurdles, winning a 2m4f Killarney novice (yielding) and a Grade 2 novice over this C&D (sft-hvy); jumped poorly and well beaten stepped up to Grade 1 company in the Turners at Cheltenham; capable of better than that and notable that he is the choice of Townend.
runners.comment: Classy on Flat, C&D Grade 2 winner, poor latest but could rebound, preferred by Townend
runners.owner: Oti Racing & The Why Knot Partnership

runners.name: Too Bossy For Us
runners.spotlight: One of three for yard; Useful on the Flat; off the mark over hurdles in a C&D maiden in February (sft-hvy); beaten 11.5l when midfield in the Supreme at Cheltenham (when Townend rode) and raced keenly before pulled up in the Fairyhouse Grade 2 won by Koktail Brut latest; overlooked by Townend here.
runners.comment: Useful on Flat, struggled up in class since C&D maiden win, overlooked by Townend

runners.name: Blake
runners.spotlight: Flat winner; won impressively on hurdles debut at Down Royal and then touched off in the Royal Bond by Koktail Brut; further behind that rival in DRF Grade 1 and beaten a neck by Le Labo in Grade 3 at Naas; still in the mix when falling at the last at Fairyhouse last time in first-time cheekpieces (discarded); can go close with a clear round.
runners.comment: Closely matched with Koktail Brut and Le Labo, go close with clear round of jumping

runners.name: Eachtotheirown
runners.spotlight: Both wins have come when ridden from the front, a Galway maiden hurdle and a Thurles handicap off 124; has come up well short in both previous runs in graded company; now tried in a hood; others preferred.
runners.comment: Has come up well short in two previous runs in graded races, hood goes on

runners.name: Free Spirit
runners.spotlight: One of three for yard; French recruit; won on second start for Daniela Mele at Dieppe (2m1f, soft); last of three over C&D in Grade 2 on Irish debut; never involved when 100-1 in the Supreme last time; major improvement required.
runners.comment: French winner, has come up short on first two runs for Willie Mullins, more needed

runners.name: Le Labo
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner; mishaps first two runs over timber, unseating at the first at Thurles then falling with the race at his mercy at Cork; put in a clear round last time in a Grade 3 at Naas (16.5f, sft-hvy) and lowered the colours of Blake by a neck; should go well again if as effective on this quicker ground (bumper win on good).
runners.comment: Failed to complete first two starts over timber, beat Blake in Gr'3 latest, go well again

races.race_name: Dooley Insurance Group Champion Novice Chase (Grade 1)

runners.name: Western Fold
runners.spotlight: Progressive in the first half of the season; won the Galway Plate, beat subsequent Savills Chase winner Affordale Fury at Gowran in October; second to Kaid d'Authie in an unsatisfactory four-runner DRF event; faded from three out in Brown Advisory; badly hampered and unseated two out in Fairyhouse Grade 1 won by Fleur In The Park.
runners.comment: Needs to recapture early-season form, Galway Plate winner and Grade 2 Gowran scorer

runners.name: Kitzbuhel
runners.spotlight: Set the early standard in the staying novice division, winning Grade 1 Kauto Star at Kempton (3m, good) at Christmas after a 2m3f beginners' win at this venue; blotted his record in Sandown Grade 1, jumped right before unseating at the sixth; redeemed himself with a stylish front-running victory in the Brown Advisory; now looks strongest of the Mullins quartet.
runners.comment: Best of the Mullins quartet judged on his Brown Advisory win atoning for Sandown failure

runners.name: Oscars Brother
runners.spotlight: Prospered after second to Western Fold on chase debut at Tipperary last May; hat-trick included small-field 3m Grade 2s at this venue and Navan; took time to warm up and left with plenty to do in the Brown Advisory, made late progress to finish fourth behind Kitzbuhel; towards the back when unseated at the 15th (Chair) in the Grand National.
runners.comment: Late progress to finish fourth behind Kitzbuhel at Cheltenham, unseated in Grand National

runners.name: Spanish Harlem
runners.spotlight: Smart staying handicapper at his best; won the Kerry National at Listowel last September and looked set for victory in the Thyestes at Gowran Park in January when unseating at the final fence; pulled up in the Bobbyjo at Fairyhouse, same outcome in the Grand National.
runners.comment: Kerry National winner last autumn, non-finisher four times since then, very risky

runners.name: July Flower
runners.spotlight: Won Grade 2 Arkle Trial over 2m at Cheltenham in November before finishing third in 2m1f event at Leopardstown in December; appeared not to stay the extended 2m4f in the Grade 2 mares' chase at the Cheltenham Festival; however, she was placed in a Grade 1 hurdle around this distance in France two seasons ago.
runners.comment: Only mare in the field, reservations about stamina and seems short of the standard needed

runners.name: Fleur In The Park
runners.spotlight: Won a 2m beginners' chase at Wexford in October; went close in a 2m Grade 2 here in November; after a break, well below his best at Thurles in March; career-best with the help of new hold-up tactics when outsider of eight in Grade 1 Fairyhouse, beating Kappa Jy Pyke; third in a Grade 1 novice hurdle here a year ago; fair chance of staying this far.
runners.comment: A revelation when winning 2m4f Grade 1 at Fairyhouse, fair chance of getting this trip

runners.name: Nowwhatdoyouthink
runners.spotlight: Point, bumper and maiden hurdle winner; off the mark over fences at this track (2m5f) in November; better effort when second to C'Est Ta Chance in a Thurles novice event over a similar trip; good effort when winning a three-runner Cork event; takes a big jump in class.
runners.comment: Fine effort when winning a three-runner Cork event, takes a big jump in class.

runners.name: Predators Gold
runners.spotlight: Smart novice hurdler in 2023-24 season before 19-month absence; returned to win a beginners' event at Navan in December, beating Slade Steel; jumping was untidy when he was beaten at odds-on by stablemate Kappa Jy Pyke here in January; challenging and going well when falling two out in the 2m4f Fairyhouse Grade 1 won by Fleur In The Park.
runners.comment: Going well when falling two out in the 2m4f Fairyhouse Grade 1 won by Fleur In The Park

runners.name: Kappa Jy Pyke
runners.spotlight: Found wanting at Grade 1 level over hurdles last season; much more accomplished as a chaser; beat a long odds-on stablemate in Thurles beginners' event; beat Jacob's Ladder readily here in January in a Grade 3 race in which Predators Gold gave a below-par display; failed to cope with Fleur In The Park at Fairyhouse.
runners.comment: Failed to cope with Fleur In The Park at Fairyhouse, helps to add depth to stable's squad

races.race_name: Goffs Defender Bumper

runners.name: Huntin Boots
runners.spotlight: E160,000 3yo; fourth foal; half-brother by Walk In The Park to recent debut bumper winner Emerald Enigma; dam is closely related to winners, notably the talented Identity Thief; probably unsuited by modest gallop when no match for a Willie Mullins-trained odds-on chance at Gowran in January; heads a five-runner squad for Gordon Elliot.
runners.comment: E160,000 3yo, probably unsuited by modest gallop when second to a Mullins runner on debut

runners.name: Dooks
runners.spotlight: E52,000; fifth foal, Westerner gelding is a brother to very useful hurdle/chase winner Amarillo Sky; should be better for a maiden hurdle outing at Clonmel; trainer has a strong hand.
runners.comment: E52,000, should be better for a maiden hurdle outing at Clonmel, trainer has a strong hand

runners.name: Adifferentballowax
runners.spotlight: E28,000 3yo; fourth foal; dam placed in a bumper and over hurdles, half-sister to high-class bumper/hurdle/chase winner Mr Mole; from a good French jumping family; trainer has stronger contenders.
runners.comment: E28,000 3yo, from a good French jumping family, trainer has stronger contenders
runners.owner: John F Doyle & R Bannerman & Mrs Sheila O'Ryan

runners.name: Strategic
runners.spotlight: E12,000 F, E11,000 Y, E85,000 3yo; 11th foal; half-brother to five winners, including useful multiple scorers Ziga Boy and Stratagem; champion point-to-point rider Barry O'Neill booked; helps to give the stable a strong hand.
runners.comment: Half-brother to five winners, Barry O'Neill booked, helps to give the stable a strong hand

runners.name: Sierrascope
runners.spotlight: E38,000 F, E72,000 3yo; ninth foal; Telescope gedling, brother to point/bumper/hurdle winner Seeyouinmydreams, half-brother to three winners including Songe (Flat/hurdle/chase) and Sandy Cay (Flat/hurdle); dam won on Flat; respected connections.
runners.comment: E72,000 3yo; dam won on the Flat and has a decent record at stud,  respected connections

runners.name: Mastertown Miss
runners.spotlight: E15,500 3yo; sixth foal; half-sister by Yeats to winners, notably Lowry's Bar (six races); made a satisfactory debut when second in a maiden hurdle at Clonmel; experience should be an asset, but this is a different sort of test.
runners.comment: E15,500 3yo; second in a maiden hurdle at Clonmel, that experience should be an asset

runners.name: Bacharach
runners.spotlight: E65,000 2yo, E200,000 3yo; tenth foal; half-brother by Nathaniel to winners, notably the talented Santini; dam 1m4f Flat/2m hurdle winner; could be a big player with Derek O'Connor aboard.
runners.comment: E200,000 3yo, half-brother to the talented Santini, noteworthy with Derek O'Connor booked

runners.name: Whispered Verdict
runners.spotlight: E11,000 Y, E24,000 3yo; fourth foal; half-brother to two German winners; dam German Group 3 winner; stable's Bacharach was a much more expensive purchase.
runners.comment: Half-brother to two German winners, stable's Bacharach was a much more expensive purchase

runners.name: Barstool Prophet
runners.spotlight: E23,000 F, E30,000 3yo; third foal; filly by Crystal Ocean out of a bumper/chase winner by Kings Theatre; cheekpieces fitted.
runners.comment: E30,000 3yo, third foal; dam a bumper/chase winner by Kings Theatre; cheekpieces fitted

runners.name: Altesse du Luy
runners.spotlight: E145,000 3yo; second foal; dam French 2m1f hurdle winner; one of two fillies in the race for the champion trainer who has won three of the last five editions; rider and owners bidding for a repeat of shared 2023 success; likely to be in the mix.
runners.comment: E145,000 3yo, trainer has won this in three of the last five years, should be in the mix

runners.name: Birdie Conway
runners.spotlight: E15,000 3yo; first foal; Jet Away filly out of an unraced sister to stable's Champion Bumper winner The Mourne Rambler, and two other useful performers, Sixshooter and She's A Star.
runners.comment: E15,000 3yo, cheap purchase, pedigree boosted by her dam's sister The Mourne Rambler

runners.name: Goliath's Sun
runners.spotlight: E23,000 Y, E42,000 3yo; fifth foal; half-sister to two Flat/hurdle winners; dam 1m4f winner by Authorized, half-sister to a 1m Listed winner; trainer saddled the third in this last year.
runners.comment: E42,000 3yo, fifth foal, half-sister to two Flat/hurdle winners; dam 1m4f winner

runners.name: Share A Dream
runners.spotlight: E88,000 3yo; third foal; half-sister to French hurdle/chase winner La Delirante; dam unraced sister to French 2m4f Grade 3 chase winner Royale Francois.
runners.comment: E88,000 3yo; third foal; half-sister to French hurdle/chase winner La Delirante

runners.name: Tacograph
runners.spotlight: E38,000 F, E20,000 3yo; third foal; dam unraced sister to point winner Royal Captain; plenty of stamina in the pedigree; rider partnered the stable's 2021 winner Adamantly Chosen.
runners.comment: E20,000 3yo, plenty of stamina in the pedigree, rider partnered the stable's 2021 winner

runners.name: Abbeyglen
runners.spotlight: E160,000 3yo; seventh foal; No Risk At All gelding, brother to useful bumper/hurdle winner Keskonrisk, half-brother to four winners including the smart Grand Sancy; dam French hurdle/chase winner; tongue-tie fitted; interesting sort for respected connections.
runners.comment: E160,000 3yo, dam has a solid record, interesting sort for respected connections

runners.name: Casino Chip
runners.spotlight: E75,000 foal, E60,000 3yo; Nathaniel gelding second foal out of Limini, a middle distance Flat winner and smart hurdle winner for Willie Mullins; family of Irish Oaks winner Vintage Tipple; strong trainer/rider combination.
runners.comment: E60,000 3yo, dam Limini was talented and versatile, strong trainer/rider combination

runners.name: Noble Indulgence
runners.spotlight: E20,000 3yo (vendor); 12th foal; half-brother by Order Of St George to six winners, including recent Grade 1 hurdles winner Zanoosh, Listed Flat scorer Paiardina, Brave Eagle (nine races over jumps), Nucky Johnson (bumper/ hurdle); dam 1m4f winner.
runners.comment: E20,000 3yo (vendor), failed to sell, good pedigree boosted lately by half-sister Zanoosh

runners.name: Gili Island
runners.spotlight: E45,000 foal, (E38,000 vendor 3yo); seventh foal; half-brother to hurdle/chase winner Mr Incredible; disappointing favourite on debut over C&D; rejected by Harry Swan now.
runners.comment: E38,000 3yo (vendor), disappointing favourite on debut over C&D; rejected by Harry Swan

runners.name: Mento
runners.spotlight: E52,000 3yo; half-brother to two French winners; finished with only one behind him at Leopardstown first time out.
runners.comment: E52,000 3yo, finished with only one behind him at Leopardstown first time out;

races.race_name: William Hill Champion Chase (Grade 1)

runners.name: Il Etait Temps
runners.spotlight: Ground versatile; not the biggest in stature but a hugely talented performer who has won eight of his 11 chase starts, including six Grade 1s; bolted up in the Champion Chase on latest, immediately bouncing back from a rare blip at Sandown; won a Grade 1 novice over C&D at this meeting last year and he's preferred by Townend; leading chance.
runners.comment: 8-11 over fences including six Grade 1s, bolted up in Champion Chase latest, big chance

runners.name: Majborough
runners.spotlight: 4-8 over fences; has a massive engine and has won three Grade 1s over fences, including when giving Marine Nationale a 19l beating in first-time cheekpieces at the DRF; however, his jumping is often erratic and the cheekpieces weren't as effective when he again jumped poorly when soundly beaten in the Champion Chase; obvious chance if he jumps well but hard to know what to expect; a risky proposition.
runners.comment: Three-time Grade 1 winner, erratic jumper at times, big chance if jumps well but risky

runners.name: Marine Nationale
runners.spotlight: The 2025 Champion Chaser arrives here a fresher horse than most after missing Cheltenham with a sore neck; returned to action at Leopardstown in December with a pleasing effort behind Solness after making an early mistake; no match for Majborough at the DRF on testing ground but he's a much better horse on a sounder surface; won this in fine style last year and could repeat the dose.
runners.comment: 2025 Champion Chaser, won this last year, fresh, gets his ground, big chance

runners.name: Blood Destiny
runners.spotlight: One of four for Willie Mullins; gained a fourth chase win in a Naas Grade 3 in February; creditable effort when a 6l runner up behind Energumene in a Fairyhouse Grade 2 on latest; 3lb worse off with that rival here and looks set to play a supporting role again.
runners.comment: Fourth chase win in Naas Gr'3 on penultimate, 6l behind Energumene on latest

runners.name: Energumene
runners.spotlight: Rolled back the years with a dominant win at Fairyhouse on latest, his first start at 2m4f for almost six years; won this in 2022 and 2023 but he's a 12yo now and Paul Townend prefers the chances of Il Etait Temps; probably vulnerable to younger legs dropped back in trip.
runners.comment: Rolled back the years on latest, won this in 2022 & 2023, vulnerable to younger legs

races.race_name: Willie Coonan Memorial INH Flat Race

runners.name: Bunker Buster
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to three winners; well supported first time out in a bumper at Navan last month; raced a bit keenly and was mastered by the Willie Mullins-trained second favourite; could make his presence felt with that run under his belt.
runners.comment: Mastered by Willie Mullins-trained second favourite when favourite on debut,  can improve

runners.name: Pretty Fortunate
runners.spotlight: E30,000 3yo; 11th foal; half-sister by Soldier Of Fortune to five winners, including the useful Regent's Stroll; dam unraced half-sister to Cheltenham Gold Cup winner Denman; one for the short list.
runners.comment: Half-sister to five winners, dam unraced half-sister to the brilliant Denman, interesting

runners.name: Hartford
runners.spotlight: E235,000 3yo; Blue Bresil gelding is a half-brother to the very useful Handstands and two other winners; could manage only third when favourite on debut at Naas in January; had 6 1/2l to spare over fourth-placed Jerpoint Abbey; Coole Cafe has boosted the form of the race and the winner Quiryn finished sixth in the Grade 1 bumper at Cheltenham.
runners.comment: E235,000 3yo, 6 1/2l in front of Jerpoint Abbey at Naas, winner took sixth at Cheltenham

runners.name: Red Seagull
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; dam an unraced half-sister to two 2m4f winners; made a good first impression over hurdles at Cork despite a few untidy jumps, ran on for second behind a Gordon Elliott-trained runner who had won an academy hurdle.
runners.comment: Made a good first impression over hurdles at Cork despite a few untidy jumps

runners.name: Gotawinnerinyou
runners.spotlight: E7,000 Y; ninth foal; half-sister to bumper and high-class hurdle/chase winner Dunvegan, winner of six races for this yard.
runners.comment: Ninth foal, half-sister to bumper and high-class hurdle/chase winner Dunvegan

runners.name: Senorita Diana
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-sister to Flat/hurdle winner Spanish John; dam 1m1f Flat winner/maiden hurdler.
runners.comment: Half-sister to Flat/hurdles winner Spanish John, dam 1m1f Flat winner

runners.name: Teofil
runners.spotlight: Seventh foal; unraced dam closely related to Irish 1,000 Guineas/Yorkshire Oaks winner Pleascach; interesting with Derek O'Connor taking the ride for Jim Bolger.
runners.comment: Unraced dam closely related to Irish 1,000 Guineas/Yorkshire Oaks winner Pleascach

runners.name: Ballyadam Star
runners.spotlight: E60,000 3yo; seventh foal; half-brother to three winners, notably Brentford Hope, who has won three races on the Flat and six over hurdles; dam French 1m3f winner; of some interest.
runners.comment: E60,000 3yo, half-brother to nine-time winner Brentford Hope, of some interest

runners.name: Blue Nude
runners.spotlight: 11th foal; half-brother to jumps winners Another Emotion and Linodargent; dam French 2m3f hurdle winner; others have stronger claims on pedigree.
runners.comment: 11th foal; half-brother to two jumps winners, others have stronger claims on pedigree

runners.name: Delamotte
runners.spotlight: E85,000 yearling; second foal; half-brother by Galiway (sire of Vauban) to French AW sprint winner Parisian Leah; dam unraced sister to 5f Group 3 winner Dibajj, half-sister to winners Black Sea (1m2f Listed) and Galvez (1m2f); has to be taken seriously despite stamina doubt.
runners.comment: Plenty of speed in his Flat pedigree, has to be taken seriously despite stamina doubt

runners.name: Eamon's Dream
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam placed over hurdles, sister to 7f 2yo winner Randall's Alannah; not one of the more obvious contenders.
runners.comment: Dam placed over hurdles/sister to a 7f 2yo winner, not one of the more obvious contenders.

runners.name: Evans Wishes
runners.spotlight: E45,000 3yo; fourth foal; half-brother by Maxios to winners Warm In Gorey (three races over hurdles) and Bay Empress (bumper); dam hurdle winner.
runners.comment: E45,000 3yo, fourth foal; half-brother by Maxios to winners Warm In Gorey  and Bay Empress

runners.name: Ignite The Moment
runners.spotlight: E37,000 F, (E30,000 vendor 3yo); second foal; dam a well-related bumper/hurdle winner; was going well when falling two out in a four-runner point at Ballycahane; has joined Paul Nolan from a good source.
runners.comment: Going well when falling two out in a point, dam a well-related bumper/hurdles winner

runners.name: Kinbara Fiftyfifty
runners.spotlight: E7,000 F; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to smart 2m5f-3m2f hurdle/chase winner Steely Addition, out of unraced half-sister to bumper and 2m-3m hurdle/chase winner Titian Blonde, in good hands.
runners.comment: First foal; dam unraced half-sister to useful hurdle/chase winner Steely Addition

runners.name: Mr Ragstone
runners.spotlight: E62,000 2yo, E165,000 3yo; ninth foal; half-brother to five winners including Black Corton (14 races) and Hugos New Horse (seven races); dam unplaced 1m4f AQPS Flat; Jody Townend aboard, with Patrick Mullins on Delamotte.
runners.comment: E165,000 3yo; ninth foal; half-brother to five winners including the talented Black Corton

runners.name: Robbie Diamonds
runners.spotlight: E17,000, E16,000 3yo; sixth foal; half-brother to winners Hills Of Glory (point/2m3f hurdle) and Three Kings (point); dam unraced half-sister to Champion Bumper and high-class 2m/2m1f hurdle/chase winner Ferny Hollow.
runners.comment: Half-brother to two winners, dam unraced half-sister to Ferny Hollow

runners.name: Rossnowlagh
runners.spotlight: E52,000 foal; first foal; dam unplaced 1m1f-1m6f (RPR 51), closely related to winners Nearly Caught (1m7f Group 2) and Katie's Reign (7f 2yo), half-sister to winners Pale Mimosa (2m Group 2) and Suailce (useful 1m4f/2m).
runners.comment: Belated debut for E52,000 foal purchase, dam has a good staying Flat pedigree

runners.name: Summer Bee
runners.spotlight: E140,000 3yo; first foal; dam useful bumper/hurdle/chase winner, half-sister to winners Tombstone and Walking On A Dream; one of three for his trainer, one of two for the owners along with the once-raced Hartford; notable jockey booking.
runners.comment: E140,000 3yo, first foal; dam useful bumper/ hurdle/chase winner, notable jockey booking

runners.name: Jerpoint Abbey
runners.spotlight: Justify filly was a USD275,000 yearling purchase; half-sister to US 6.5f AW/1m dirt winner Lady Lowery; dam unraced half-sister to US Grade 3 1m AW winner Speightster, out of unraced sister to BC Distaff winner Dance Smartly; not a typical recruit to jumping.
runners.comment: Unsuited by heavy on debut, needs to reverse form with Hartford, not out of the question

runners.name: Its My Twist
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to a bumper winner; unraced dam out of half-sister to Champion Hurdle winners Morley Street and Granville Again; never in the hunt on debut at Kilbeggan last Friday.
runners.comment: Good pedigree but was never in the hunt on debut at Kilbeggan last Friday

races.race_name: Weatherbys Racing Bank Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Runman
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to the classy 1m-1m2f performer Running Lion and this colt was nicely on top late on in a useful 1m novice at Salisbury in October; could easily outclass this opposition and progress to better things.
runners.comment: Debut winner; could easily outclass this opposition and progress to better things

runners.name: Vitality
runners.spotlight: Went down by 3l in a five-runner 1m Southwell maiden (16-1) after making the running; a 3yo filly taking on the males but could prove the main threat to Runman.
runners.comment: Went down by 3l in a five-runner 1m Southwell maiden (16-1) after making the running
runners.owner: Miss D Finkler, A Herd, Dr P Holloway 1

runners.name: Hattie's Comet
runners.spotlight: 62,000gns yearling out of a 7f/1m winner (Listed; RPR 100) and half-sister to French winners Spherical (7f) and I Am Incredible (5.5f); best watched unless the betting suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: 62,000gns yearling out of a 7f/1m winner (Listed); heed the market signals

runners.name: Thomas Picton
runners.spotlight: 65,000euros yearling; 66-1 when 18th of 25 in a 1m Newbury maiden ten days ago; this race has far less depth.
runners.comment: 65,000euros yearling; 66-1 when 18th of 25 in a 1m Newbury maiden ten days ago

runners.name: Get Outta Here
runners.spotlight: Beaten in the region of 7l at Deauville (7f) and Kempton (1m); likely to be seen in a better light when handicapping.
runners.comment: Beaten in the region of 7l at Deauville (7f) and Kempton (1m)

runners.name: Mick The Hat
runners.spotlight: Handicaps could beckon after this following down-the-field runs on the AW (at about 1m; 16-1/80-1).
runners.comment: Handicaps could beckon after this following down-the-field runs on the AW

runners.name: Hengest
runners.spotlight: Bought for £1,800 after one run for Andre Fabre; beaten 28l in one run over hurdles for Jamie Snowden and was last at Yarmouth on recent stable debut (1m).
runners.comment: Three runs for three different yards; finished last at Yarmouth only recently
runners.owner: Gee Gees Racing, P Gleeson & N Barnes

runners.name: Fizzy Bear
runners.spotlight: Modest fifth at Kempton before finishing just ahead of Thomas Picton when both were down the field at Newbury ten days ago.
runners.comment: Modest fifth at Kempton before finishing just ahead of Thomas Picton at Newbury

runners.name: Pureis King
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner but well beaten in two 1m races on the Kempton AW and unlikely to be winning this.
runners.comment: Bumper winner but well beaten in two 1m races on the Kempton AW

races.race_name: Betfred Blue Riband Trial Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: New Zealand
runners.spotlight: By Frankel; dam won the Group 3 Albany (6f) as 2yo and bred Group-placed Agenda (1m4f) for this yard; clung on for 7f maiden win at the Curragh on second start but was a better horse in the autumn, not having much to race against when involved in a scramble for second in a Group 3 at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) then bogged down by the class rise and/or very soft ground in the Group 1 Criterium International at Saint-Cloud; likely to do better as a 3yo over this longer trip; good chance with sights lowered.
runners.comment: Close up in Group 3 in October; fair 5th in French Group 1 after; good claims up in trip
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier/Michael Tabor/Derrick Smith/Peter M Brant

runners.name: Balzac
runners.spotlight: Winning siblings include Claymore, a Group 3 winner at 1m2f for this yard; lots to like about his persevering third of seven in a good 1m Newmarket maiden (22-1; ridden by 7lb claimer) on debut but he didn't need any more than that form when making all on Kempton AW (1m) this month; Derby entry who should make a better 3yo but how much better will be clearer after today.
runners.comment: Lots to like about 1m Newmarket debut; didn't need to improve to win 1m AW novice

runners.name: Menzies
runners.spotlight: One sibling was a Graded winner at 1m3f and 1m1f in US; took a step forward from 7f debut on Kempton AW when making the running and not for catching over 1m there in February; had more to do under a penalty at Southwell since and he fell short so now relies on big improvement for the step up in trip.
runners.comment: Wide-margin winner of 1m AW novice on 2nd start; needs plenty more than subsequent defeat

runners.name: Saxon Street
runners.spotlight: 170,000euros yearling; unraced dam's winning siblings include Raheen House (1m5f; Group 3) and Shraaoh (Australian 2m Group 1); a warm order for Chelmsford AW debut (1m) in December and although workmanlike rather than impressive in beating rivals who mostly look handicappers in the making, he did finish with purpose; should leave that form well behind over a more suitable trip for yard that's won five of the last ten runnings; entered in the Dante and the Derby.
runners.comment: 170,000euros yearling; came home strongly when winning AW debut (1m); new trip will suit

runners.name: Pearl River
runners.spotlight: All five races on good or slower; middling form on first three starts but was all the better for the step up to 1m2f on final two runs and his stamina was much in evidence when forging clear in a Nottingham handicap (good to soft), despite running green; needs to have come on a ton over the winter to play a part and the track and good to firm ground are other queries.
runners.comment: Stout staying win in 1m2f handicap (good to soft) on final 2yo start; much more needed

runners.name: Rebel Rocker
runners.spotlight: Derby entry; nibbled at in the market for 1m debut on Kempton AW in November where he made a good impression with the way he made up ground to win quite convincingly; his rivals had some limitations though and although this one should improve he is asked a big question today.
runners.comment: Made a good impression when winning AW debut (1m) but the bare form was ordinary

races.race_name: Lilley Plummer Risks City & Suburban Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Asmen Warrior
runners.spotlight: Runner-up last July-October on three of his first four starts for this yard and he again finished second on this month's reappearance at Thirsk (1m4f, good), albeit no match for the winner; he could build on that latest effort and is not discounted.
runners.comment: Runner-up on four of his five starts since joining this yard and he's not discounted

runners.name: Dangerman
runners.spotlight: Won two in a row at Sandown (1m2f, good) last summer when these blinkers went on and third there (1m2f, soft) next time; appeared unsuited by the step up to 1m4f at Newmarket in October; this lightly raced 4yo is in excellent hands and could rediscover his progressive ways with a big run today.
runners.comment: Improved for these blinkers last summer and could rediscover progressive ways on comeback

runners.name: Sallaal
runners.spotlight: Made it three wins from his first four starts when scoring on handicap debut at Hamilton (8.3f, good) last July; his progress stalled in two Listed races subsequently but this lightly raced 4yo has been gelded since and the return to better ground could be a positive, along with the step up in trip; retains potential for a top trainer who won this in 2018 and 2019; respected.
runners.comment: Made it 3-4 at Hamilton last July; his progress then stalled but he retains potential

runners.name: Duke's Command
runners.spotlight: Won at Lingfield (AW) in December on first crack at 1m2f and raced wide when sixth of 14 at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) last month; this two-time turf winner remains unexposed at today's trip and is not ruled out.
runners.comment: Raced wide when sixth of 14 at Newcastle; remains unexposed at 1m2f and not ruled out

runners.name: Rathgar
runners.spotlight: Beat 13 rivals to win at Newbury (1m2f, good) ten days ago on his second start back from some time off; back up 4lb but he's just 1lb higher than when second in this last year and he's a solid contender.
runners.comment: Last year's runner-up who won at Newbury ten days ago; solid contender

runners.name: Parlando
runners.spotlight: Two AW wins in December and returned to turf with 3l third of eight at Pontefract (1m2f, good) last Tuesday; might not be far away but there's a suspicion that this exposed 7yo might find a few too good; cheekpieces go on.
runners.comment: 3rd of eight at Pontefract when back on turf; suspicion that he might find a few too good

runners.name: Aurel
runners.spotlight: Absent since midfield finish in Golden Gates Handicap at Royal Ascot (1m2f, good to firm) last June; gelded since; however, he was 2-3 previously, the wins coming in a maiden at Kempton (1m, AW) and a novice at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm), and he may well have more to offer for his respected trainer.
runners.comment: Absent since Royal Ascot last June; 2-3 previously and may well have more to offer

races.race_name: Weatherbys Global Stallions App Great Metropolitan Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Topteam
runners.spotlight: Really likeable sort who scored three times on good ground (1m2f/1m4f) last term, twice coming out on top in tight finishes; gelded before again showing a fine attitude to pick up Kempton handicap (1m4f, AW) on his reappearance three weeks ago; likely won't go down without a fight for his ever-growing fan club.
runners.comment: Scrapper who won three times on turf last term and again on AW return; tops the list

runners.name: Asgard's Captain
runners.spotlight: Last year's winner (good) who ran well in two other appearances over C&D; did well to win at York (1m4f, good to firm) under John Egan in August and arrives here fit after three creditable efforts at Meydan; firm contender.
runners.comment: Won this last year; arrives fit after creditable efforts in UAE and must be considered

runners.name: Nunc Est Bibendum
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish mare who secured an AW hat-trick once upped to 2m across December and January but suffered odds-on reversal when dropped to 1m6f in bid for the four-timer; drops further in trip returned to turf (had form on grass in Ireland) on this first start for three months; others more persuasive.
runners.comment: Secured AW hat-trick up at 2m over the winter; something to prove at this trip on turf

runners.name: Sing Us A Song
runners.spotlight: Won heavy-ground Goodwood maiden at two and it was a convincing success on last year's reappearance/handicap debut at Sandown (1m2f, good) one year ago; disappointed at Royal Ascot and Glorious Goodwood subsequently but had been gelded when back on the scoresheet at Haydock (1m6f, good to soft) in September; drops in trip for this comeback but not fully exposed.
runners.comment: Winning 1m2f return last year; signed off with another win (1m6f) in September; unexposed

runners.name: Night Breeze
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced in last year's Northern Dancer over C&D (good; likely softer) on sole start here; also won twice at Ascot (good to firm) last term; back to form on last two starts, latterly when beaten 2l at Musselburgh (also 1m4f, good) on Sunday; should make his presence felt.
runners.comment: Sound surface and 1m4f ideal; back in form lately, including at Musselburgh on Sunday

races.race_name: Betfred 'Nifty 50' Handicap

runners.name: Assaranca
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on her eighth start as a juvenile when landing Nottingham nursery (good, made all) over this trip in October; not disgraced in Listed company next time but weakened over 1m2f (good to soft) on her reappearance one month ago; might do better back at this distance with the run behind her.
runners.comment: Nursery winner; can be excused easy-ground reappearance over 1m2f; not readily dismissed

runners.name: Timber Twelve
runners.spotlight: Overcame a wide stall when springing 28-1 surprise at Kempton in September and kept unbeaten record when defying a penalty on his return at Southwell (also 1m, AW) five weeks ago; will likely be going up in trip soon enough but brings plenty of potential to this turf/handicap debut.
runners.comment: 2-2 and brings plenty of potential to this turf/handicap debut; must be considered

runners.name: Montague Menace
runners.spotlight: Gelded ahead of his AW debut when readily landing Lingfield maiden (7f) on last month's return; will probably need more on this switch back to turf/handicap debut but it's still early days and he warrants consideration.
runners.comment: Gelded when winning AW maiden last month; should have more to offer on handicap debut

runners.name: Mayaada
runners.spotlight: Busy as a juvenile when her nine runs yielded one win; that did come here (7f, good) in August but was quiet on last two starts and virtually tailed off on last month's AW comeback; return to this venue might help, and a handicapping case can be made, but otherwise has plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Course maiden (7f, good) winner last term but seems to have lost her form; down the list

runners.name: On Message
runners.spotlight: Promise on first two starts (good to firm/soft) and she made all on AW debut at Wolverhampton (7f) in October; should do better at this trip and beyond (holds Oaks and Irish Oaks entries) this term; interesting on her reappearance and handicap debut.
runners.comment: Promise on turf; made all on AW in October; interesting on reappearance and handicap debut

runners.name: Terranoble
runners.spotlight: All starts around 1m on AW; only made his debut in December but that was an eyecatching effort and he improved on third start to land Wolverhampton novice in January; potential to do better again on this turf/handicap debut.
runners.comment: All starts around 1m on AW; improved to win January novice; respected on turf/h'cap debut

races.race_name: Betfred 'In Celebration Of Mark Friar' Handicap

runners.name: Mr Baloo
runners.spotlight: 7f nursery winner here (good; has won on good to firm) in 2023; landed seventh AW win when scoring at Lingfield (1m; first-time cheekpieces, retained) a fortnight ago; has won off higher marks than this and must be considered on his return to turf.
runners.comment: 7f nursery winner here who won (AW) in cheekpieces last time; retains handicapping scope

runners.name: Tribal Rhythm
runners.spotlight: Returned from seven months off to win this race from off the pace last year; couldn't build on that in four subsequent starts last season but was back down to that winning mark when slightly unlucky second at Bath (1m, also good) on his resumption three weeks ago; of firm interest.
runners.comment: Last year's winner who looked unlucky when second on this month's comeback; every chance

runners.name: Tronido
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner who won at Sandown in August and followed up by 6l over C&D (soft; acts on good) in September; just 2lb above that latter winning mark but there hasn't been much encouragement in three AW starts this term.
runners.comment: Knows how to win but little encouragement in three starts this term; mark fine

runners.name: Shafdar
runners.spotlight: All starts on AW for this yard and he justified cramped odds when easily landing four-runner Chelmsford maiden (1m) in February; promising handicap debut when second over that C&D the following month and can be excused a lesser effort in highly competitive Good Friday event three weeks later; unexposed, particularly on this surface, and he warrants consideration.
runners.comment: Unexposed and can be excused latest; warrants consideration on first turf start for yard

runners.name: Sweet Reward
runners.spotlight: Seven wins include five off higher marks than this and one at this track (1m2f, good to soft; has won on good) in 2023; this trip might be on the short side but he went close on his reappearance in 2024 (1m2f) and scored off 2lb higher on his comeback last term (1m2f); not readily dismissed.
runners.comment: This trip is on the short side but he won on last year's reappearance and is 2lb lower

runners.name: Kaleido
runners.spotlight: Placed on soft and fast ground in his first three maidens (7f/1m) before landing the odds with comfortable win at Yarmouth (7f, good) in July; made favourite for handicap debut but weakened to finish ninth at Glorious Goodwood (1m, heavy; Knights Gold third) a fortnight later; bought for 21,000gns in October and subsequently gelded; retains plenty of potential ahead of this seasonal/stable debut.
runners.comment: Unexposed and retains potential ahead of this seasonal/stable debut; has been gelded

runners.name: Knights Gold
runners.spotlight: Five handicap wins (7f to 1m; four in a hood) in a productive 2025, including on a fast surface, and he looked unlucky when third of 15 at Glorious Goodwood (1m, heavy; Kaleido ninth) in July; probably needed reappearance in March and likely didn't stay 1m2f when never competitive three weeks later; this mark demands a return to his best, though.
runners.comment: Productive last year but yet to get going this term; mark demands a return to his peak

runners.name: Amused
runners.spotlight: Ex-French gelding who was unraced at two; made all to land Vichy claimer (very soft) in August and went mighty close for this yard in Leicester seller (good) in October; 25-1 and raced wide before solid fifth of 12 at Kempton (also 7f, AW) when last seen in December; cheekpieces enlisted for second go at this sort of trip and he's not ruled out on return.
runners.comment: Solid AW fifth in December; cheekpieces on for this return and he's not ruled out
runners.owner: P And Mrs L Rowe And John Turner

runners.name: Organ
runners.spotlight: Completed a hat-trick over 7f/1m on good/good to firm across May and June last year; form tailed off markedly from August onwards but he returns just 2lb above his last winning mark; market support would bring him into the picture.
runners.comment: Landed hat-trick last term before form tailed off; nothing wrong with this mark on return

runners.name: Carnival Day
runners.spotlight: Respectable fourth in the Great Metropolitan on last year's card; one win last year but it did come here in July and he was a respectable second at this track (also 1m2f, good to soft; has won on good) in September; below par over 1m on his final start in October and this trip again asks a question; market might help ahead of his comeback.
runners.comment: 1m2f winner here in July; this trip raises a question on return; market might help

races.race_name: Follow @attheraces On X Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Mr Ubiquitous
runners.spotlight: Strike-rate of 6-16 so he knows how to win and only 3lb higher than for his half-length reappearance success at Kempton (7f, AW); every chance.
runners.comment: Only 3lb higher than for his half-length reappearance success at Kempton (7f, AW)

runners.name: Dancing Terror
runners.spotlight: First and second in two 7f novices at Lingfield the winter before last, but having been off for a year, he's trailed home last in 1m/7f handicaps (tried a hood and blinkers last time); makes turf debut with lots to prove.
runners.comment: Career started well but has shown nothing in two runs after an absence

runners.name: Me Tarzan
runners.spotlight: Four wins divided between AW and turf; ideally positioned through the race when narrowly scoring at Kempton (7f) but this consistent 4yo has clear claims.
runners.comment: Ideally positioned through the race when narrowly scoring at Kempton (7f); clear claims

runners.name: Diamont Katie
runners.spotlight: Back from a wind operation when winning comfortably last week at Pontefract (6f, good); carries a penalty but should be hard to beat if as good again, as returning to 7f won't hinder her at all.
runners.comment: Back from a wind operation when winning comfortably last week at Pontefract; penalised
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Lxxiv & M Hulin

runners.name: Way To Dubai
runners.spotlight: Has won only once since making his British debut in October 2023; has threatened plenty of times but has been very poor in last two races.
runners.comment: Won only once since making his British debut in October 2023; very poor last two races

runners.name: Noble Guest
runners.spotlight: Had 15 races; second career win when prevailing narrowly over C&D in September and following efforts weren't too shoddy; still on a competitive mark but he does lack a recent run.
runners.comment: Dual winner still on a competitive mark but he does lack a recent run

races.race_name: Free Tips Daily On attheraces.com Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Nevasca Cinza
runners.spotlight: All runs over 6f; left the Russell/Scudamore stable for 100,000gns in October (gelded afterwards) after competitive shows at Carlisle and Hamilton; produced another on yard debut in a good-looking novice at Leicester; sets the standard and should have a lot more to offer.
runners.comment: Sets the standard on what he's shown in three races and should have a lot more to offer

runners.name: Creative Queen
runners.spotlight: 800,000euros breeze-up 2yo; a beaten favourite in both her races, in a three-runner event last year and only midfield at Kempton (7f) three weeks ago; still early days but it's been an underwhelming start.
runners.comment: Still early days but it's been an underwhelming start from this well-related filly

runners.name: Firewalker
runners.spotlight: Cost 360,000gns as a foal; beaten at 1-4 on debut and cut out quickly when again prominent in the betting at Doncaster (7f) one month ago; held a 2,000 Guineas entry that day but not now; may step up but has to.
runners.comment: Beaten at 1-4 on debut; cut out quickly when again prominent in the betting at Doncaster

runners.name: Izzy Fast
runners.spotlight: Modest RPRs in her first two races and doesn't look up to winning a maiden.
runners.comment: Modest RPRs in her first two races and doesn't look up to winning a maiden

runners.name: Unique Procedure
runners.spotlight: Beaten about 12l at Nottingham ten days ago (6f, good) when a 100-1 chance; the hood is retained.
runners.comment: Beaten about 12l at Nottingham ten days ago (6f, good) when a 100-1 chance

runners.name: Shining Guest
runners.spotlight: Beat one home on debut here (7f) before trailing in last at Ascot (6f); absent since July.
runners.comment: Beat one home on debut here (7f) before trailing in last at Ascot (6f); absent since July

races.race_name: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Handicap

runners.name: Lucky Sevens
runners.spotlight: Easily best form when he responded well for pressure to lead late on over C&D last week (first-time cheekpieces); that was from 1lb out of the weights so he's 5lb higher here with a penalty, but that doesn't make him well in.
runners.comment: Best form yet when he responded well for pressure to lead late on over C&D last week

runners.name: Montu
runners.spotlight: Finished close-up when placed three times in a row in February/March from 9.4f to 1m4f, and Alice Bond removes 7lb today; however, he was beaten about 6l when only seventh of 11 at Wolverhampton (1m4f) last night.
runners.comment: Placed three times in a row in February/March but only seventh of 11 last night

runners.name: Darkest Red
runners.spotlight: Gelded after last season and was only 9-2 for his reappearance in a 1m handicap at Lingfield (AW) where he was running on after a wide trip; raised in distance and the dam stayed this far; respected.
runners.comment: 9-2 for first run after being gelded and was running on after a wide trip

runners.name: Private Project
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden who has finished behind Montu in her last two races; more needed.
runners.comment: Eight-race maiden who has finished behind Montu in her last two races; more needed

runners.name: Sporty Socks
runners.spotlight: Seven defeats include four handicaps and she was well held when twice placed; still commands some respect in this modest handicap given she's shown ability and has dropped to a career-low mark.
runners.comment: Off a career-low mark in this modest handicap and she has been placed before

runners.name: Keep It Classic
runners.spotlight: One of the lesser lights at his strong yard but may show improvement now handicapping upped in distance (from 7f; dam won over this far) and fitted with headgear; market could be informative.
runners.comment: May show improvement now handicapping, upped in distance (from 7f)

runners.name: Sea Mehalah Run
runners.spotlight: Very minor level of form in her qualifying races, all over 7f (tongue tied in the latest); like a few in here, she's entitled to raise her profile now sent into a low-grade handicap.
runners.comment: She's entitled to raise her profile now sent into a low-grade handicap

runners.name: Cracking Anna
runners.spotlight: Beaten between 12.5l and 15.5l in maiden/novice races on the AW (9.4f/7f); nicely bred and disappointing should she not be a great deal more competitive off this low a mark.
runners.comment: Nicely bred and disappointing should she not be more competitive off this low a mark

runners.name: Out In The Cold
runners.spotlight: Poor so far, including two handicaps at about 1m on the AW when big prices; opposable.
runners.comment: Poor so far, including two handicaps at about 1m on the AW when big prices; opposable

runners.name: Karios
runners.spotlight: Left James Ferguson for 1,600gns after showing precious little in four 1m races last autumn (maidens/novices); handicapped accordingly if nothing else; has been gelded.
runners.comment: Left James Ferguson for 1,600gns after showing precious little in four 1m races

runners.name: Fletcher
runners.spotlight: Has beaten one rival in three starts (7f-1m); has been gelded ahead of this handicap debut and makes sense to check the betting; stable also represented by Darkest Red.
runners.comment: Poor so far but gelded ahead of this handicap debut and makes sense to check the betting

races.race_name: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap

runners.name: Sword Of Wessex
runners.spotlight: 0-8 for Charlie Fellowes; four runs for current yard, the pick when a close fifth over 1m5f at Lingfield (AW) three runs back; pretty poor since and he's now had lots of chances over various distances.
runners.comment: Poor in last two races and he's now had lots of chances over various distances

runners.name: One Million Dreams
runners.spotlight: Not a lot to show for his runs on the Flat or over jumps for previous yard but he's back with Nick Littmoden for whom he did well in Flat handicaps last year; on a competitive mark.
runners.comment: He's back with Nick Littmoden for whom he did well in handicaps last year

runners.name: Pershalla
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced for a 4yo; seemingly all the better for her comeback run, and the fitting of a hood, when finding the most to lead near the line at Kempton (1m4f, AW) 20 days ago; back up 4lb but in form and probably capable of better.
runners.comment: Back up 4lb for the AW Kempton win but in form and probably capable of better

runners.name: Atlantic Sunset
runners.spotlight: 1-14 for Charlie Johnston, the win coming off 7lb higher at Newmarket last summer (1m4f); that was in a new visor and he struggled afterwards, including in blinkers that are now reapplied; joined this yard for 1,000gns.
runners.comment: Lost his way for previous yard after last summer's sole win; joined this one for 1,000gns

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Lady Mariko
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who did well to win over 6f at Kempton (AW) 22 days ago as this is probably more her trip; however, the rise in grade off a 6lb higher mark asks questions of her.
runners.comment: Did well to win over 6f at Kempton (AW) 22 days ago as this is probably more her trip

runners.name: Shallow
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner who lurks on a good mark; hasn't been at her best this year, though January's run was okay and if new cheekpieces do the trick then she could be dangerous.
runners.comment: If new cheekpieces do the trick then she could be dangerous off this mark

runners.name: Ironist
runners.spotlight: Course winner last September over 6f on soft ground when 3lb lower; ran okay after her break last week but this is much tougher than usual.
runners.comment: Ran okay last week after her break but this is much tougher than usual

runners.name: Bridget's View
runners.spotlight: 8lb higher than for her only handicap win but was not beaten far in a blanket finish at Lingfield (7f, AW; stable debut) and the tongue-tie tried when below her best last time is swiftly removed; better now than when last seen on turf earlier in career; should go well.
runners.comment: Tongue-tie removed after last time; previous close fifth at Lingfield brings her into this

runners.name: Lady Manzor
runners.spotlight: In first-time cheekpieces she was a closing fourth last time at Lingfield (7f, AW) in a race she won the previous year; all three wins have come on the AW but has comparable turf form (0-3); enters the reckoning.
runners.comment: Signs of returning to form in the new cheekpieces last time at Lingfield

runners.name: Miss Nightfall
runners.spotlight: Has only won a novice in 13 starts but has some classy form to her name, notably a close fifth in a Group 3 over this trip last September; has contested Listed or Group races in her last four starts (first attempt at 1m on return) and her handicap form is very solid.
runners.comment: Highly tried on last four starts and her handicap form is very solid

runners.name: Havana Pusey
runners.spotlight: Last season went well in 6f/7f handicaps and was only 3l away in a Group 3 on one occasion; however, that campaign ended badly and her reappearance at the Craven meeting was no better.
runners.comment: Last campaign ended badly and her reappearance at the Craven meeting was no better

races.race_name: Get Raceday Ready Handicap

runners.name: Moonhall Lass
runners.spotlight: Failed to win in 22 Irish starts but made a positive debut for James Owen when a close third at Southwell (6f, AW); likely contender with cheekpieces back on (ran okay in them when tried once in Ireland).
runners.comment: Made a positive debut for James Owen when close third at Southwell (6f, AW)

runners.name: Griselda
runners.spotlight: Won three nurseries for George Scott; not the same filly now but handicapped accordingly and went close at Southwell (6f, AW) 17 days ago.
runners.comment: Has regressed but handicapped accordingly and did go close on the AW last time

runners.name: Edergole's Gift
runners.spotlight: Only 3-35 so hard to win with and since going close on the AW in January she's lost her way; others preferred.
runners.comment: Since going close on the AW in January she's lost her way; others preferred

runners.name: Sir Rodneyredblood
runners.spotlight: Chelmsford specialist who ran another sound race there on final start in March; only 1-17 on turf but has course form and this mark gives him a chance.
runners.comment: Only 1-17 on turf but has course form and this mark gives him a chance

runners.name: Chiedozie
runners.spotlight: Former Charles Hills inmate who is now 0-24; well beaten in both his races following a long absence.
runners.comment: Now 0-24 and well beaten in both his races following a long absence

runners.name: No Gain
runners.spotlight: Tried all sorts of trips for previous yard but still quite lightly raced; tongue-tie and cheekpieces fitted for her two runs for current yard, only 2l away on the first occasion (6f) and the drop back to 5f found her out last time; could be interesting returned to 6f with blinkers given a go.
runners.comment: 5f appeared too sharp last time; interesting returned to 6f with blinkers given a go

runners.name: Top Biller
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who got upset in the stalls and was slowly away last time at Wolverhampton; his previous two runs were also below best form and easy lead looks unlikely with Grand Citadel in the field.
runners.comment: Had excuses last time; front-runner for whom an easy lead looks unlikely

runners.name: Grand Citadel
runners.spotlight: Only win was at 7f and four of her last five races have been at 1m; stuck around here 17 days ago despite developing a clear lead and expect similar tactics back at 6f.
runners.comment: Ran well over 1m here last time after going off fast; expect same tactics back at 6f

runners.name: Dion Baker
runners.spotlight: Has been struggling on the AW but he's 0-30 in that scene and all four wins have been on turf, albeit over 7f; lurks on a dangerous mark but this trip is not ideal.
runners.comment: Lurks on a dangerous mark but this trip is not ideal, with all his wins over 7f

races.race_name: Follow @attheraces On Instagram Handicap

runners.name: Go Lockers Go
runners.spotlight: Can take a strong hold but has still run well in both his handicaps at Chelmsford (5f/6f) since being gelded and was nicely on top last time (hood returned); dropping back to 5f shouldn't hinder too much in this weak a race; raised 6lb.
runners.comment: 6lb higher than for his win on the AW but he was nicely on top at the finish
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing D Turner B Roberts

runners.name: Zoustar Dreams
runners.spotlight: Competitive in 5f/6f AW maidens but backward steps since then, finishing last on handicap debut when tried in blinkers latest; retains some potential with the headgear now left off.
runners.comment: Disappointing on handicap debut but retains some potential with headgear now left off
