runners.name: Blueaway
runners.spotlight: All runs on the AW and in a hood; sent off 15-8 favourite for her handicap debut (5f) but was in rear throughout and has since left Rod Millman for 6,000gns; has to be of some interest.
runners.comment: New yard; 15-8 for handicap debut in February but was in rear throughout

runners.name: Saeculamation
runners.spotlight: Chelmsford winner at 6f; more exposed than some in here but he ran well two days ago at Nottingham having been given every chance.
runners.comment: He ran well two days ago at Nottingham having been given every chance

runners.name: Takincareofbizness
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on his three starts on AW last year but gelded prior to his handicap debut at Nottingham (5f) in which he finished fourth in a field of 17; interesting that he went off favourite that day and probably capable of better.
runners.comment: Sent off favourite for handicap debut and he finished fourth of 17; should run well

runners.name: Sea Her Excel
runners.spotlight: Last season's third came in a three-runner race; beaten 10l on Sunday's handicap debut at Nottingham (6f; 10-1); the hood tried then is removed.
runners.comment: Beaten 10l on Sunday's handicap debut at Nottingham (6f; 10-1); hood removed

runners.name: Sweet Echo
runners.spotlight: August's debut at Windsor was encouraging but she's not beaten a rival since then; makes handicap debut after 150 days away.
runners.comment: August's debut was encouraging but she's not beaten a rival since then; handicap debut

races.race_name: Celebrate With Sponsorship At Southwell Racecourse Handicap

runners.name: Brother Dave
runners.spotlight: Returned from break with hard-fought C&D win in January and has held his form pretty well since, albeit his latest run was a shade disappointing; current mark is workable; no surprise if he features.
runners.comment: Has largely remained in good form since hard-fought C&D win in January; could have a say

runners.name: Kamekist
runners.spotlight: Unable to land a telling blow when favourite here (6f) this month but was close third off much-reduced mark here two starts ago and ought to be suited by today's return to 7f; tongue-tie removed after one use; it might pay to give him another chance.
runners.comment: Didn't fire here last time but was close third off much-reduced mark here last month

runners.name: River Wharfe
runners.spotlight: 11-time winner, most recently from off the pace over C&D last month; not in same form on either 8.6f start at Wolverhampton since, though, and might still be a shade too high in the weights.
runners.comment: Came from off the pace for C&D win in March; held twice since; others look better treated

runners.name: Hint Of The Jungle
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 7f (once here) in February and confirmed his wellbeing with respectable fifth at Kempton (6f) this month; probably doesn't have much margin for error off current mark but ought to be in the shake-up again.
runners.comment: Won twice over 7f (once here) in February and recent 6f run confirmed his wellbeing

runners.name: Fistral Beach
runners.spotlight: Dual 7f winner at Wolverhampton this year, latterly when holding on well in first-time cheekpieces last month; probably bettered that form when second from wide stall there 18 days; respected in current mood.
runners.comment: Held on well at Wolverhampton last month and good second from wide stall there since

runners.name: English Lady
runners.spotlight: Dual 7f Polytrack winner; did well to finish third over C&D on recent seasonal debut, having endured a wide trip from stall 12, and is still quite lightly raced after only 12 starts; high on the list.
runners.comment: Did well to finish third over C&D on recent seasonal debut, having raced wide; big player

runners.name: Reenie's Dream
runners.spotlight: Quirky maiden who has often shown a tendency to race too freely and who lost all chance when virtually refusing to race on penultimate start; did well to take fourth over C&D last month, but remains a risky option.
runners.comment: Ran on well for fourth over C&D last month but this quirky maiden remains a risky option

runners.name: Dolly's Delight
runners.spotlight: Won over an extended 7f on turf last summer and was subsequently placed over 6f on Tapeta; made underwhelming reappearance this month and has wide draw today but might have needed that recent outing and may by aided by the new tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Made low-key return this month but might come on for the run; tongue-tie now added
runners.owner: The Good Racing Company Ltd & Partner

runners.name: Ballybaymoonshiner
runners.spotlight: Gained all four wins on Polytrack, the latest last May; returned from break with poor run in December and has wide draw for this first start since; others have much stronger claims.
runners.comment: Returned from break with poor run in December and has wide draw for this first start since

runners.name: King Sharja
runners.spotlight: Scored off 2lb higher over C&D last April and was also on target off today's mark on turf last summer; ran well when fresh last year and has a fighting chance if fully tuned up for this reappearance outing.
runners.comment: Went close on last season's reappearance and subsequently won twice (once over C&D)

runners.name: Split Elevens
runners.spotlight: Ten-time AW winner; just a respectable fifth at Wolverhampton (7f) this month but remains on a workable mark and can't be ignored.
runners.comment: Ten-time AW winner; just a respectable fifth this month but remains on a workable mark

runners.name: Saisons d'Or
runners.spotlight: Returned from break with three down-the-field runs this year but his fourth of ten over C&D three weeks ago was a big step back in the right direction and he's another 2lb lower today; considered each-way.
runners.comment: Latest run was a big step back in the right direction and he's another 2lb lower today

races.race_name: Wright Vigar Handicap

runners.name: Nad Alshiba Snow
runners.spotlight: Not yet off the mark but she hasn't always had the rub of the green for this yard and her latest run might have been needed; has run well on Wolverhampton Tapeta but this is her first course run so she does have a slight surface query.
runners.comment: Maiden; hasn't always had the rub of the green for this yard; first course run

runners.name: Beneficiary
runners.spotlight: Can lead; has won at both sprint trips on this course; two good runs in classified events during February but below best at Wolverhampton last month and was beaten nearly out of sight back over 5f on turf last time; first-time visor replaces blinkers; others higher up the list.
runners.comment: In good form without winning in February; below best last two starts, particularly latest

runners.name: Man On A Mission
runners.spotlight: Seven AW wins have come elsewhere but he has often run well over C&D and did so again when only narrowly failing to end a losing run here on Saturday; had no problem with returning to 5f for the first time in over a year and a likely candidate.
runners.comment: Seven AW wins elsewhere but acts well over C&D and nearly won on Saturday; can go well

runners.name: Marcus
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden but there isn't much between him and Arlington on C&D form in October and although he was beaten before the 6f trip became an issue at Chelmsford in February he can't be ruled out on return from a break.
runners.comment: Maiden; capable of being involved on best form in 2025, such as a C&D run in October

runners.name: Arlington
runners.spotlight: Three front-running C&D wins between last May and January; below best on last two starts but he might just have needed the recent sharpener following a break and can't be passed over too lightly.
runners.comment: Three front-running C&D wins between last May and January; might have needed latest run

runners.name: Stash The Cash
runners.spotlight: Can lead; on a tempting mark relative to past turf form but he's 0-7 on the AW and has been well beaten since only just failing against Arlington over C&D in January; couldn't count on refitted blinkers, replacing a visor, returning him to form.
runners.comment: Low mark but 0-7 on AW and no repeat of close 2nd to Arlington in January in 2 runs since
runners.owner: D Morgan And K J Parris

runners.name: The Carligation
runners.spotlight: Huge prices in just five starts, showing a glimmer of ability but finished remote on handicap debut at Lingfield in 2024; off since.
runners.comment: Glimmer of ability in five starts but finished remote on handicap debut in 2024; off since

runners.name: Fumbleinthejungle
runners.spotlight: Winless in 12 starts and it was an uphill grind in handicaps last year; wore aids on last two starts but not today; one to watch on return from break.
runners.comment: Winless in 12 starts; uphill grind in handicaps last year; one to watch back from break

races.race_name: Book The Bistro In Sherwood Restaurant Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Society Kiss
runners.spotlight: Both starts for Ralph Beckett last year were at Ascot (5f, good to firm), making a winning debut in May (form worked out well) before finishing in midfield in the Queen Mary the following month; half-sister to a couple of AW winners; market informative on stable debut (same owner) after 314 days off.
runners.comment: 1-2 for Raph Beckett as a 2yo; market informative on stable debut after 314 days off

runners.name: Queen Sana
runners.spotlight: Showed ability when in the frame in four of her first five starts, but she didn't run very well on her handicap debut here (6f) three weeks ago; doesn't seem to be progressing and needs the drop to 5f to make a difference; stable won this last year.
runners.comment: Doesn't seem to be progressing and needs the drop to 5f to make a difference

runners.name: Reigning Queen
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 5f/6f winner (including 2yo/Group 3; RPR 110), closely related to Swedish 6f winner Siphon River (including 2yo), half-sister to winners Mr Minerals (7f 2yo/1m), Insomnia (7f) and Ormskirk (6f-1m4f); appealing pedigree and makes her debut in a winnable race.
runners.comment: Appealing pedigree and makes her debut in a winnable race

runners.name: Magic Lady Mae
runners.spotlight: Third of ten on her Chelmsford return (7f, Polytrack) in March, but hasn't gone on in two subsequent starts; needs a resurgence down in tip.
runners.comment: Third of ten on her Chelmsford return in March but hasn't gone on in two subsequent starts

runners.name: Shes Got A Brother
runners.spotlight: 100,000gns 2yo; sent off 11-1 when making all on her debut over C&D 19 days ago; stable doesn't have many go in first time and the runner-up has won since, so she could be above-average; respected.
runners.comment: Made all on her debut over C&D 19 days ago; runner-up has won since; respected

runners.name: Lavender Bloom
runners.spotlight: 26,000euros foal; 11,000euros yearling, 35,000gns breeze-up 2yo; half-sister to winners So Chic (7f/1m AW; RPR 69) and Diego Spirit (Italian 6f 2yo/1m); dam placed 7f in France, sister to 7f-1m6f Flat/2m hurdle winner, half-sister to Italian 5f Listed winner Gamgoom; probably only of interest if backed on debut.
runners.comment: Probably only of interest if backed on debut

runners.name: Dance Of Angels
runners.spotlight: In the frame in two starts over 5f at Sandown (good) and Nottingham (good to soft) as a 2yo; stable has had a couple of winners recently and worth a market check on return from seven months off.
runners.comment: In the frame in two starts over 5f on turf as a 2yo; worth a market check on return

runners.name: Dolo's Star
runners.spotlight: Well held in both starts, finishing over 6l behind Shes Got A Brother over C&D on the second occasion; 6lb pull not enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Well held in both starts including when over 6l behind Shes Got A Brother over C&D

runners.name: Gwen Tennyson
runners.spotlight: 12-1 when beating one home on her Newcastle debut (6f, Tapeta) in December; major improvement required.
runners.comment: Beat one home on her Newcastle debut in December; major improvement required

races.race_name: Come Racing For Free 18th June Handicap

runners.name: Jack Andrea
runners.spotlight: In fine form in recent months and bids for a course hat-trick; had half a length to spare over Arth's Gold over this trip last time and is now 3lb worse off, but something appears to have clicked; major player.
runners.comment: In fine form lately and bids for a course hat-trick; something appears to have clicked
runners.owner: J Rayment, J Stephenson & R Eddery

runners.name: Night Bear
runners.spotlight: Just 1-30 on the AW, but the win came over C&D and he was only beaten a head by Jack Andrea here (1m3f) last month; 5lb pull makes him a player even though his losing run is mounting up.
runners.comment: C&D winner and beaten just a head by Jack Andrea here last time; 5lb better off

runners.name: Eagles Whistle
runners.spotlight: Rattled up a hat-trick in 0-50 classified events on Tapeta for Daragh Bourke (7f-9.5f) in January; has run with credit in her last three starts for this yard, but still has to conclusively prove she wants this far.
runners.comment: Has run with credit in her last three starts, but still has to prove she wants this far

runners.name: Arth's Gold
runners.spotlight: 0-13 and latest effort on turf was modest, but he was only beaten half a length by Jack Andrea over this C&D the time before (pair clear); 3lb better off and every chance if bouncing back.
runners.comment: 0-13, but only beaten half a length by Jack Andrea over C&D this month and 3lb better off

runners.name: Electric Bass
runners.spotlight: Forced a dead-heat at Kempton (1m4f, Polytrack) in November, but was subsequently demoted so is technically 0-11; return to this trip should suit, but he has been off for 130 days; market useful.
runners.comment: Return to this trip should suit, but has been off for 130 days; market useful

runners.name: Tupero
runners.spotlight: Showed improvement when off the mark at Newcastle (1m4f, Tapeta) in February and ran at least as well when chasing home a subsequent winner at Catterick (1m4f, good) this month; still unexposed after just six starts and high on the list.
runners.comment: Won at Newcastle in February and runner-up to a subsequent winner at Catterick this month

runners.name: Spec Of Light
runners.spotlight: Patchy form since gaining his sole success here (1m) 13 months ago, but has run well on a couple of occasions over middle-distances on this track during that period; each-way claims if producing his best.
runners.comment: Has run well on a couple of occasions over middle-distances here; each-way claims

runners.name: Lednikov
runners.spotlight: Often a front-runner and has won here six times, the latest (1m6f) in March; finished well behind a couple of these over C&D last time, though, so has a bit to find.
runners.comment: Has won here six times, but finished well behind a couple of these over C&D last time

runners.name: Artavian
runners.spotlight: Sole success came at Windsor (11.4f, heavy) in October 2024 and ended the last campaign with a fair second of seven at Nottingham (1m2f, soft); off six months and has run well fresh, but others still make greater appeal.
runners.comment: 1-15; off six months and has run well fresh, but others still make greater appeal
runners.owner: G E Bassett & P R Chamings

runners.name: Shaffron
runners.spotlight: Hinted at ability in five starts on turf/Polytrack last year without progressing; drops in grade on her return from 197 days off, but may be best watched unless attracting support.
runners.comment: 0-5; drops in grade on her return from 197 days off, but may be best watched

races.race_name: Conference & Events At Southwell Racecourse Handicap

runners.name: Sullivan Bay
runners.spotlight: Returns to the AW after another modest effort over hurdles, but was twice runner-up on Polytrack before that; both wins have come on that surface, but he ran well to finish a close third behind Payment Plan over this C&D in November 2024; each-way claims again.
runners.comment: Dual Polytrack winner, but ran well when third over C&D in November 2024; each-way claims

runners.name: Charlie Darling
runners.spotlight: Dual turf winner in Ireland who was having his third start for this yard when third of eight over C&D this month; drops in grade and every chance if building on that performance.
runners.comment: Third over C&D earlier this month; drops in grade and every chance if building on that

runners.name: Regal Glory
runners.spotlight: Has run some good races on turf/Tapeta this year, but a record of 1-37 sounds a note of caution and she is unraced beyond 1m6f.
runners.comment: Record of 1-37 sounds a note of caution and she is unraced beyond 1m6f

runners.name: Cold Henry
runners.spotlight: Returns to the Flat after showing mixed form over hurdles; placed three times in seven visits here including over this trip, but others are more compelling.
runners.comment: Placed three times in seven visits here, but others are more compelling

runners.name: Payment Plan
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner in November 2024, but finished miles behind Charlie Darling on his return from 11 months off back here 26 days ago; the market should indicate whether a much better effort is anticipated.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner, but well beaten on his reappearance here earlier this month; watch market

runners.name: Colinski
runners.spotlight: Sole win came at Lingfield (11.6f, good; first-time cheekpieces) for James Owen last June, but out of the frame in two starts on the AW since returning for this yard last month; didn't enjoy a clear run on either occasion, but he still needs to step up.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in two starts on the AW since returning for this yard last month

runners.name: Onlyriversrunfree
runners.spotlight: 0-7; best effort when third of 12 in a 0-50 classified event at Lingfield (1m4f, Polytrack; first-time cheekpieces) last month; has something to prove over this far, though.
runners.comment: 0-7; third at Lingfield last month, but has something to prove over this far

runners.name: Crystal Guard
runners.spotlight: Dual 1m4f winner on Tapeta elsewhere; placed three times from six starts over this sort of trip on Tapeta and could make the frame from just 2lb above his last winning mark.
runners.comment: Placed three times from six starts over this sort of trip on Tapeta; could make the frame

runners.name: Apache Eagle
runners.spotlight: 0-15 but has been placed in his last two starts over 1m6f here, the latest when second of 11 on Saturday; 1lb out of the weights this time, but still each-way material.
runners.comment: 0-15 but finished runner-up over 1m6f here on Saturday; each-way material

runners.name: Fashionelle
runners.spotlight: Some good performances last year as when beaten a short head off this mark over C&D in November; needs to better her two efforts since returning last month, though.
runners.comment: Some good efforts last year, but needs to better her two starts since returning last month

runners.name: Different Drum
runners.spotlight: Twice ran poorly over C&D during the winter, but has winning form at Newcastle (2m, Tapeta) and was successful over hurdles this month; a player if transferring that form back to the Flat.
runners.comment: Successful over hurdles this month; a player if transferring that form back to the Flat

races.race_name: Ladies Day Sunday 16th August Handicap

runners.name: Dr Ali
runners.spotlight: Doubled his tally when reeling in the long-time leader at Wolverhampton a month ago (6f); that was a classified race but his mark was left untouched, so only 2lb higher than for his previous win; has form here.
runners.comment: Doubled his tally when reeling in the long-time leader at Wolverhampton a month ago

runners.name: Mount Ruapehu
runners.spotlight: The hat-trick bid came up shy but at Wolverhampton and he seems better here, the scene of his two previous wins; certainly not handicapped out of it.
runners.comment: The hat-trick bid came up shy but at Wolverhampton and he seems better here

runners.name: Melissa Honey
runners.spotlight: 12-race maiden who was sixth of nine here 23 days ago after a break; she wouldn't be the obvious answer.
runners.comment: 12-race maiden who was sixth of nine here 23 days ago after a break

runners.name: East Tyrone
runners.spotlight: Can miss the break and tends to pull hard; still deserves some respect after a recent third over C&D.
runners.comment: Tends to pull hard; still deserves some respect after a recent third over C&D
runners.owner: Alan And David Fairhurst And P Kirby

runners.name: Tommytwohoots
runners.spotlight: Last run was too bad to be true and had made a positive start for this yard, and he's closely matched with Dr Ali; possibilities.
runners.comment: Last run was too bad to be true and had made a positive start for this yard

runners.name: Pinjarra
runners.spotlight: Has won three 6f events on AW since August, one here; last run at Pontefract forgiven as he was drawn wide but he was also below par here the time before when behind Mount Ruapehu.
runners.comment: Drawn wide last time but also below par here the run before when behind Mount Ruapehu

runners.name: Mu Mu Land
runners.spotlight: 6f AW maiden winner as a 2yo; 0-8 in handicaps and showed no benefit for wind surgery at Pontefract.
runners.comment: 0-8 in handicaps and showed no benefit for wind surgery at Pontefract

runners.name: Queensmaite
runners.spotlight: Third in a 5f maiden here on debut but it's been a quiet start in handicaps (here) and now goes in cheekpieces.
runners.comment: It's been a quiet start in handicaps (here) and now goes in cheekpieces

runners.name: South Shore
runners.spotlight: Can miss the break and/or pull hard; has performed okay in two runs after a break and each-way claims should he give himself a chance.
runners.comment: Can miss the break and/or pull hard; has performed okay in two runs after a break

runners.name: Angel Of England
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-26; shown nothing in two runs after wind surgery and too risky to contemplate.
runners.comment: Record of 1-26; shown nothing in two runs after wind surgery and too risky to contemplate
runners.owner: The Jocks And The Geordies II

races.race_name: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Cill Mocheallog
runners.spotlight: Not the easiest to predict but came from off the pace for a second career win on his first visit to Wolverhampton in March (7f; 2lb lower); breakthrough win in January was over C&D.
runners.comment: Breakthrough win in January was over C&D and went in again at Wolverhampton last month

runners.name: Shamacid
runners.spotlight: No wins in 11 but his first run after a gelding operation and in a tongue-tie when going down narrowly in a close finish at Kempton (7f); mark untouched so every chance with a repeat.
runners.comment: Having been gelded, he went down in a close finish at Kempton; same mark here

runners.name: Phoenix Of Dreams
runners.spotlight: Wins have been over 6f on turf and 7f on the AW (Newcastle); has been running well for a while and latest effort back at Newcastle was better than it looks.
runners.comment: Running well for a while and latest effort back at Newcastle was better than it looks

runners.name: Receipt
runners.spotlight: Sharp pedigree but started out in bumpers; no match for the winner in a C&D novice last time but that's something to build on now handicapping.
runners.comment: Third in a C&D novice last time and that's something to build on now handicapping

runners.name: Dapper Gee Gee
runners.spotlight: Better than ever when readily seeing off 11 rivals over C&D in January and it was over 1m when finishing midfield in a 22-runner handicap at Doncaster; considered.
runners.comment: Better than ever here in January; his Doncaster run is readily forgiven

runners.name: Wish This
runners.spotlight: Signed off last season with a win over 6f at Newcastle and, although only sixth here after a break 17 days ago, he only faded late on; could be happier over this extra furlong.
runners.comment: Only faded late on here after a break and he could be happier over this extra furlong

runners.name: Stoneywell
runners.spotlight: The third in a Leicester novice last October is his standout run; 80-1 for a C&D handicap here in March and never counted (first-time hood).
runners.comment: 80-1 for a C&D handicap here in March and never counted (first-time hood)

races.race_name: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Ancient State
runners.spotlight: 3-3 over 5f, including here, and he took his form to a new level with a comfortable Southwell success last month; up 6lb and now tackles Class 5 company but his ceiling is yet to be established.
runners.comment: 3-3 over 5f; easy Class 6 win at Southwell last month; ceiling yet to be established

runners.name: Spendmore Lane
runners.spotlight: Two 5f wins this year, making all at Wolverhampton on the second occasion off a 9lb lower mark; her ability to overcome this mark isn't in question but the start continues to be an issue and others look safer.
runners.comment: Two 5f AW wins this year but she's odds-on to get it wrong at the start & that's the worry

runners.name: Aleppo Pepper
runners.spotlight: One promising run in Ireland in January 2025; she was returning from 435 days when a modest third of five in a Southwell maiden 19 days ago; not solid now handicapping.
runners.comment: One good run in Ireland in January 2025; can build on recent return but she needs to

runners.name: Mick's Spirit
runners.spotlight: Prolific C&D winner, most recently when making all 54 days ago; a 3lb rise won't stop him if in the same form and he may not have to work too hard to lead; major player.
runners.comment: Multiple C&D winner; may not have to work too hard to secure the lead; dangerous again

runners.name: Lequinto
runners.spotlight: No AW win since December 2021 but there have been some good runs on both Polytrack and Tapeta this year, including C&D, and yesterday's 6f second was another sound display; wasn't helping his rider under pressure on that occasion and opposable for win purposes.
runners.comment: The odd good run on AW this year, the latest when second here (6f) yesterday; others safer

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Scheffler
runners.spotlight: Shaped nicely over 7f here on debut, keeping on well in the closing stages; failed to build on it when last of nine at Wolverhampton 11 days ago (6f); should be more potent over a mile but the widest stall isn't optimal.
runners.comment: Promising 3rd over 7f here on debut; backward step dropped to 6f latest; drawn widest

runners.name: Get This In
runners.spotlight: Showed clear signs of ability in two 7f runs as a 2yo, beaten 3l into sixth at Kempton when last seen 188 days ago; gelded since then; bred to appreciate the longer trip; open to considerable improvement this year.
runners.comment: Promise in two 7f runs last year; been gelded; plenty more to come at 1m+; key contender

runners.name: Rogue Defence
runners.spotlight: 16,000gns yearling, resold 30,000gns yearling; eighth foal; half-brother to winners Meraas (6f including AW/dirt/Group 3; RPR 109), Global Hero (1m turf/8.6f AW; 92) and Winning Point (6f AW; 84); dam 1m Group 3 winner (108); already gelded; starting out in a winnable race and worth a market check.
runners.comment: 30,000gns yearling; useful pedigree and he starts out in a winnable race; check betting

runners.name: Pace Attack
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in two 1m maidens at Kempton this spring; unlikely to come into his own until tackling middle-distance handicaps.
runners.comment: Unplaced in two 1m maidens at Kempton this spring; likely one for handicaps after this

runners.name: Roi De Coeur
runners.spotlight: First foal of an Italian 8.7f/1m1f winner (RPR 92), herself a half-sister to Listed-placed winners Cima Fire (7f 2yo-1m3f) and Giausar (1m2f, placed up to 1m7f); starts out in a winnable race and he needs a market check.
runners.comment: Dam a fair winner in Italy; betting to guide on debut

runners.name: Lissahane Lass
runners.spotlight: Big prices and poor form in two bumpers this spring; hard to recommend.
runners.comment: Big prices and poor form in two bumpers this spring; hard to recommend

runners.name: Champion Lawman
runners.spotlight: 44,000euros yearling; seventh foal; brother to 7f-8.5f winner Panama Red (including 2yo/Listed/US; RPR 102), half-brother to three winners including Power Of States (1m-11.5f including 2yo/AW; 106) and Key Witness (7f/1m including AW; 88); dam maiden (83); enough paper appeal to warrant a second look.
runners.comment: 44,000euros yearling with 4 winning siblings; enough paper appeal to warrant a close look

runners.name: Kennington
runners.spotlight: 22,000gns yearling; third foal; half-brother to 8.6f AW/1m2f turf winner Dashinwhitesargent (RPR 82); dam 7f/1m winner (100), half-sister to useful winners Showout (6f 2yo Listed) and Landwade Lad (1m2f/1m3f); one of three runners for the stable.
runners.comment: 22,000gns half-brother to a winner out of a useful winning mare; yard run three here
runners.owner: Mrs M T Bevan & A Bevan

runners.name: Seraglio Point
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-brother to winners Wyoming (6f 2yo; RPR 89), Post Rider (1m/8.3f; 83) and Geelong (8.6f-11.4f including 2yo/AW; 76); dam 1m4f winner (including AW; 102); bred to have a bright future but it is likely to be over longer trips than this; stable run three.
runners.comment: Half-brother to three winners out of a useful 1m4f winner; one of three runners for yard

runners.name: Veld
runners.spotlight: Big prices and only minor promise in two fillies' novice events at around 1m this spring; others appeal more for win purposes.
runners.comment: Minor promise in two fillies' novice events this spring (around 1m); longer-term prospect
runners.owner: Sir Richard Pilkington & Mrs S Rogers

runners.name: Annahita
runners.spotlight: Kept on for a never-dangerous seventh of 13 at Kempton in November (1m); open to improvement this year but such progress is a must.
runners.comment: Never-dangerous seventh of 13 at Kempton in November (1m); needs a good surge of progress
runners.owner: Dr Jamal Ahmadzadeh & Mrs D Swinburn

runners.name: Leia Antilles
runners.spotlight: Modest form in two runs over shorter last summer; absent 267 days and the step up in trip isn't enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Modest form in two runs over shorter last summer; improvement essential

races.race_name: Follow @attheraces On Instagram Handicap

runners.name: Alasrae
runners.spotlight: He has won three of his last four starts (good second on the other occasion) yet has only gone up 8lb; finished with a flourish to win over C&D on Good Friday and he is a leading contender despite his wide draw.
runners.comment: Thriving now and he did well to win over C&D on Good Friday; drawn wide but a key player

runners.name: Akabusi
runners.spotlight: Closely matched with Alasrae on C&D form from Good Friday and his effort there can be marked up given he was poorly drawn; can be given a pass for Bath last time (hampered) and he has yet to run a poor race over C&D; more exposed than some but still commands respect.
runners.comment: His three peak efforts have come in his three C&D runs; exposed but likely to go well

runners.name: Comprador
runners.spotlight: He has finished third in each of his novice/maiden runs, with the first and third coming over today's C&D; cheekpieces which debuted last time are retained; makes his handicap debut with his trainer banging in the winners.
runners.comment: Third in his three qualifying runs; makes handicap debut for a yard in flying form

runners.name: Rogue Messiah
runners.spotlight: Ran out the ready winner of a 6f novice on his racecourse debut last summer; disappointed at Thirsk one month later but he was back on the up with a good second at Kempton on his return three weeks ago (conceding 5lb to a promising winner); remains capable of better and he is well drawn to attack; respected.
runners.comment: May have faced tough task trying to give 5lb to a promising one at Kempton; unexposed

runners.name: Nifty
runners.spotlight: Always behind after an awkward start at Wolverhampton last month; even her peak 2yo efforts, which included two wins, need bettering if she is to take this.
runners.comment: Low-key return to action last month; even her 2yo best needs bettering

runners.name: Little Jaybee
runners.spotlight: All runs at 5f; won a Wolverhampton fillies' maiden last June on her second start; good second at Nottingham one month later but she has found it much harder work since, most recently finishing last of eight at Wolverhampton four weeks ago; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Steps up to 6f for the first time and it needs to have a positive effect
runners.owner: Mrs A L Lofts & Mrs Nicky Scott Knight

runners.name: Merlier
runners.spotlight: Promising start to his career, albeit in weak small-field AW events, before a hefty defeat at Thirsk on his handicap debut ten days ago; return to AW can help but not sure he's well treated.
runners.comment: Two AW wins last month in small fields; well held at 14-1 on h'cap/turf debut; not solid

runners.name: Manly Fireball
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on his debut (6.5f, good) but he has run well in three AW maidens since; moves into handicaps with the potential for rmore but he will need to step forward to defy this mark.
runners.comment: Three promising runs on AW since a low-key turf debut; needs more to defy this mark

runners.name: Eternal Solace
runners.spotlight: C&D winner as a 2yo; two promising efforts this year, shaping nicely at Kempton before chasing home her hugely progressive stablemate Al Najashi at Newcastle on Good Friday; hit with a 3lb rise but she is still a key player now back down in class; stable also run Bone Marra.
runners.comment: Two promising efforts this year, the latest in hot race; this C&D winner has solid claims

runners.name: Bone Marra
runners.spotlight: 2-2 on AW, the wins coming on his racecourse debut (5f) and then after a three-month break in November (7f; tongue tied first time); given that ability fresh a 171-day absence is of little concern but a 5lb rise for beating a small field when last seen isn't obviously generous; stable also represented by Eternal Solace.
runners.comment: 2-2 on AW (5f and 7f); unlikely to want for fitness but this mark demands a personal best

races.race_name: Free Tips Daily On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Luna Beaux
runners.spotlight: The winner of two of her five starts for her new stable, both over 6f at Wolverhampton; found out by the drop to 5f at Bath three weeks ago; could resume her progress back at 6f.
runners.comment: Two Wolverhampton wins (6f) this year; 5f too sharp latest; not fully exposed just yet

runners.name: Dusk Damsel
runners.spotlight: She hinted at ability in three maiden runs over further last month; improvement possible dropping to 6f for her handicap debut but the handicapper has taken no chances.
runners.comment: Three maiden runs over further were not devoid of promise; opening mark looks tough enough
runners.owner: Ms H N Pinniger And Partner

runners.name: Calabrian Soldato
runners.spotlight: Good run when second at Chelmsford on his nursery debut in December (5f; cheekpieces first time); struggled since and hopes rest on the new tongue tie giving him a lift.
runners.comment: Second on nursery debut (5f) in December but no progress since; easy enough to swerve

runners.name: No Claims Bonus
runners.spotlight: Yet to kick on from her Wolverhampton third last September (5f) but she wasn't able to show what she could do at Nottingham on her recent return, having nowhere to go as the race was unfolding; steps up to 6f and remains capable of better.
runners.comment: No luck in running at Nottingham on her return; steps up in trip with untapped potential

runners.name: Enter Sandman
runners.spotlight: He had been threatening a bigger effort prior to last week's breakthrough success at Newcastle (5f), a race where he dug deep to scramble home in a blanket finish (0.75l split the first five home); not well in under a penalty but in form and stepping back up to 6f should be fine.
runners.comment: Scrambled home in a blanket finish at Newcastle last week (5f); penalty demands more

runners.name: Sun Path
runners.spotlight: Poor form in three 6f novice events at Kempton; bred to do a fair bit better at some point.
runners.comment: Poor form in three 6f novice events at Kempton; bred to do a fair bit better at some point

runners.name: Power Cut
runners.spotlight: Down the field in three novice/maiden runs on AW since October; charted a wide course and still looked green at Wolverhampton when last seen eight weeks ago; open to improvement in handicaps but he is drawn widest this time; betting to guide.
runners.comment: Likely he'll step forward now handicapping but drawn wide today; betting instructive

runners.name: Saddle Up
runners.spotlight: Low-level form in three AW maiden/novice runs this year; bred to do much better at some point but he looks the stable second string today.
runners.comment: Possible improver now handicapping but he looks the stable second string today

runners.name: Due Destiny
runners.spotlight: Poor form in four runs for Archie Watson last year; the new hood needs to make a big difference on her seasonal and stable debut.
runners.comment: Poor form for Archie Watson last year; hooded for stable/seasonal debut

runners.name: Mortubo
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in three runs in the second half of 2025, recording her best RPR over C&D when last seen 196 days ago; may do better this year but would want to see market support before getting involved.
runners.comment: Best RPR came over C&D when last seen in October; opening mark not generous

races.race_name: Download The At The Races App Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Knights Charge
runners.spotlight: 100,000euros yearling who is related to winners from 5f to 1m5f; the penny dropped late on at Kempton in December (1m; 10-1) when he was doing his best work late on; should be very competitive.
runners.comment: Produced all his best work late on when third over 1m at Kempton in December

runners.name: Liveinthelight
runners.spotlight: 290,000gns yearling; showed more on her reappearance when fifth of eight at Kempton (1m3f) but she posted a modest RPR; remains best watched.
runners.comment: Posted a modest RPR when fifth of eight at Kempton (1m3f) last month
runners.owner: Lady Bamford , Mrs Collins & Bv Sangster

runners.name: Cyrano De Bergerac
runners.spotlight: By the stable's star miler Palace Pier and this colt cost 150,000gns as a yearling; second foal; dam 1m4f AW winner (RPR 78); kicks off in a modest race and would be hard to resist if at all strong in the betting.
runners.comment: By the stable's star miler Palace Pier and this colt cost 150,000gns as a yearling

runners.name: Mr Bollinger
runners.spotlight: 40-1 and was beaten a shade under 10l at Nottingham three weeks ago (1m2f); could be one for further down the line.
runners.comment: 40-1 and was beaten a shade under 10l at Nottingham three weeks ago (1m2f)

runners.name: Be Hopeful
runners.spotlight: 95,000euros foal; brother to 7f 2yo winner Bold Impact (RPR 87) and half-brother to winners Keeper Of Time (6f-1m; Group 3; 101) and Maggie's Delight (2m1f; 85); dam Italian Listed-placed 5.5f-1m winner; gelded ahead of debut; market can guide.
runners.comment: 95,000euros foal; brother to 7f 2yo winner Bold Impact; market can guide

runners.name: Shopaholic
runners.spotlight: 2,000gns 2yo; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Ski Lift (1m1f/1m2f) and Try Out (1m4f); can only be watched on this belated debut.
runners.comment: 2,000gns 2yo; first foal out of an unraced dam; can only be watched on belated debut

runners.name: Crimson Dawn
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on three visits to Kempton, over 1m, 7f and 1m3f; no appeal.
runners.comment: Tailed off on three visits to Kempton, over 1m, 7f and 1m3f; no appeal
runners.owner: Ben Pay And Carter Roofing Solutions Ltd

runners.name: Master Dancer
runners.spotlight: Hurdle/chase winner who has offered very little in his two runs on the Flat.
runners.comment: Hurdle/chase winner who has offered very little in his two runs on the Flat

runners.name: Leonato
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to French/Australian 1m1f-1m2f winner Barazin; very green when beaten 20l at Newcastle in November (1m; 11-2) and could easily leave that form behind now gelded; market informative.
runners.comment: Quiet debut in November but could easily leave that form behind now gelded
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap

runners.name: Criminal
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden and out of sorts so far this year; had little chance the way the race panned out in a 1m handicap here two weeks ago but he was keeping on well at the finish, boding well for today's return to 1m2f; drops in class too and he's of interest.
runners.comment: Uninspiring formfigures this year but latest run was OK & he's down in class here; chance

runners.name: Hello Cotai
runners.spotlight: Formerly trained by Adam West; two wins over 8.5f at Epsom in 2024; several good runs in France last year, including a 9.5f AW win at Chantilly; starts out for Jim Boyle on a feasible mark but he is eligible for weaker races than this one; absent 284 days.
runners.comment: Returns to Britain on a dangerous mark but he has been absent for 284 days

runners.name: Voix De Bocelli
runners.spotlight: Well beaten over hurdles three weeks ago and that followed a disappointing run at Kempton; his earlier AW form brings him into the reckoning but that was over further; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Below par on Flat/over hurdles on his last two starts; this winter's best came over 1m4f

runners.name: Romantic Spirit
runners.spotlight: 4-11 on AW and only 1lb higher than for last October's front-running win at Southwell (1m3f); lower draw would have been ideal but she has the ability to feature.
runners.comment: 4-11 on AW and on a fair mark still; has the ability to feature despite wide draw

runners.name: Joseph
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 16th attempt when overcoming adversity to win a C&D handicap in February; similar form when second at Kempton last month and subsequent turf flop easy enough to forgive (yet to show anything on grass); better expected back on AW and with Billy Loughnane booked.
runners.comment: C&D win, after being badly hampered early, in February; return to AW a plus; respected

runners.name: Penfolds Grange
runners.spotlight: Had wind surgery in December and he has run three good races since, finishing fourth over C&D before two runner-up efforts at Kempton (1m); should remain competitive.
runners.comment: Three good runs have followed wind surgery; should remain competitive

runners.name: Etretat
runners.spotlight: Four AW wins, including C&D, and returns from a six-month break on a manageable mark; more good runs than bad at this track and he's not discounted.
runners.comment: C&D winner; returns from six months off on a dangerous mark; not discounted

runners.name: Onslow Gardens
runners.spotlight: 19-race maiden; ran some good races in defeat for this yard last year, including when last seen in October (1m2f, good to soft); withdrawn at the start having got upset in the stalls ahead of this month's intended return (10-1 at the time of withdrawal); others preferred for win purposes.
runners.comment: Exposed 19-race maiden; on a fair mark but others appeal more for the win
runners.owner: Dr M Voikhansky & Mrs S Voikhanskaya

runners.name: Lenny's Spirit
runners.spotlight: Just 1-28 on AW but he went desperately close to doubling that tally in an amateur rider's race over C&D last month, only headed in the dying strides (first run for Adam Kirby); 2lb higher today; one to consider with new headgear tried.
runners.comment: Modest record on AW but he ran a cracker over C&D on last month's stable debut; key player

runners.name: Blue Wonder
runners.spotlight: Recorded RPRs of 70 (twice) and 75 in her three novice/maiden runs last season; struggled badly in four handicap runs thereafter, dropping 9lb in the process; may bounce back at some point but she is risky back from a 202-day break.
runners.comment: Her novice/maiden runs were promising but she struggled in handicaps after; off 202 days

races.race_name: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap

runners.name: Wild Thoughts
runners.spotlight: Some sprint promise in early 2025; pedigree promised improvement for 1m on handicap debut at Redcar (good to firm) last week and it came to pass in some style; makes a quick return under a penalty but likely still well in; big chance on AW debut.
runners.comment: Ready winner last week when upped to 1m on handicap debut; likely well in under penalty

runners.name: Timely Salute
runners.spotlight: Fair 7f debut on Chelmsford AW but never involved in two 1m starts after, most recently in a  Yarmouth novice 17 days ago; his pedigree suggests that trips beyond 1m may be ideal but still a likely type for handicaps and the betting can guide.
runners.comment: Pedigree suggests he may need further than 1m but market can guide on handicap debut

runners.name: Labiche
runners.spotlight: Late foal who hinted at some ability over 7f and 1m last summer; gelded since; has been off since August but he's well connected and may well show more now handicapping for a yard among the winners of late.
runners.comment: Minor 2yo form at 7f and 1m; off since August; open to improvement now handicapping
runners.owner: T A Frost & Simon Broke & Partners

runners.name: Betty Lemon
runners.spotlight: Minor 2yo form, finding nothing new when switched to handicaps (1m; turf and AW) in the autumn and has plenty to find on her return; hood added.
runners.comment: Minor 2yo form, including in 1m handicaps; has plenty to find on return; hood added

runners.name: Katalyst
runners.spotlight: Best efforts over 7f elsewhere but she didn't look as if the 1m trip was beyond her when fifth of nine on handicap debut at Southwell in December; overall form suggests improvement needed for the return to Polytrack.
runners.comment: Worth another go at 1m but overall form suggests improvement needed off current mark

runners.name: Bennyworth
runners.spotlight: Ran well for a long way on second and final start for James Owen (6f, AW) but always behind  over C&D on yard debut; gelded since; handicaps should offer more scope but his opening mark doesn't look overly generous.
runners.comment: Some promise in qualifying runs; gelded since latest; opening mark doesn't look generous

races.race_name: Prix de Suresnes (Listed Race) (Turf)
Prix de Suresnes (Listed Race) (Turf)
Betfred Blue Riband Trial Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: New Zealand
runners.spotlight: By Frankel; dam won the Group 3 Albany (6f) as 2yo and bred Group-placed Agenda (1m4f) for this yard; clung on for 7f maiden win at the Curragh on second start but was a better horse in the autumn, not having much to race against when involved in a scramble for second in a Group 3 at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) then bogged down by the class rise and/or very soft ground in the Group 1 Criterium International at Saint-Cloud; likely to do better as a 3yo over this longer trip; good chance with sights lowered.
runners.comment: Close up in Group 3 in October; fair 5th in French Group 1 after; good claims up in trip
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier/Michael Tabor/Derrick Smith/Peter M Brant

runners.name: Balzac
runners.spotlight: Winning siblings include Claymore, a Group 3 winner at 1m2f for this yard; lots to like about his persevering third of seven in a good 1m Newmarket maiden (22-1; ridden by 7lb claimer) on debut but he didn't need any more than that form when making all on Kempton AW (1m) this month; Derby entry who should make a better 3yo but how much better will be clearer after today.
runners.comment: Lots to like about 1m Newmarket debut; didn't need to improve to win 1m AW novice

runners.name: Menzies
runners.spotlight: One sibling was a Graded winner at 1m3f and 1m1f in US; took a step forward from 7f debut on Kempton AW when making the running and not for catching over 1m there in February; had more to do under a penalty at Southwell since and he fell short so now relies on big improvement for the step up in trip.
runners.comment: Wide-margin winner of 1m AW novice on 2nd start; needs plenty more than subsequent defeat

runners.name: Saxon Street
runners.spotlight: 170,000euros yearling; unraced dam's winning siblings include Raheen House (1m5f; Group 3) and Shraaoh (Australian 2m Group 1); a warm order for Chelmsford AW debut (1m) in December and although workmanlike rather than impressive in beating rivals who mostly look handicappers in the making, he did finish with purpose; should leave that form well behind over a more suitable trip for yard that's won five of the last ten runnings; entered in the Dante and the Derby.
runners.comment: 170,000euros yearling; came home strongly when winning AW debut (1m); new trip will suit

runners.name: Pearl River
runners.spotlight: All five races on good or slower; middling form on first three starts but was all the better for the step up to 1m2f on final two runs and his stamina was much in evidence when forging clear in a Nottingham handicap (good to soft), despite running green; needs to have come on a ton over the winter to play a part and the track and good to firm ground are other queries.
runners.comment: Stout staying win in 1m2f handicap (good to soft) on final 2yo start; much more needed

runners.name: Rebel Rocker
runners.spotlight: Derby entry; nibbled at in the market for 1m debut on Kempton AW in November where he made a good impression with the way he made up ground to win quite convincingly; his rivals had some limitations though and although this one should improve he is asked a big question today.
runners.comment: Made a good impression when winning AW debut (1m) but the bare form was ordinary
